lessons.text: You can't not act, so act well.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing deeds brings neither actionlessness nor perfection; Nature compels everyone to act ceaselessly.
lessons.old_text: You cannot escape action, even for a moment.

verses.text: Man does not reach actionlessness by not performing actions; nor does he attain perfection by mere renunciation.
Verily, no one can remain for even a moment without performing action; for everyone is made to act helplessly, indeed, by the qualities born of Nature.

lessons.text: Don't fake discipline while your mind wanders.
lessons.rationale: Stilling the limbs while brooding on sense-objects is hypocrisy; true yoga controls the senses and still acts.
lessons.old_text: Don't fake restraint while your mind chases pleasures.

verses.text: He who, restraining the organs of action, sits thinking of the sense-objects in his mind, he of deluded understanding is called a hypocrite.
But whoever, controlling the senses by the mind, O Arjuna, engages himself in Karma Yoga with the organs of action, without attachment, he excels.

lessons.text: Doing something beats doing nothing.
lessons.rationale: Perform your bounden duty, for even sustaining the body is impossible through inaction.
lessons.old_text: Do your appointed work; action beats inaction.

verses.text: Perform your bounden duty, for action is superior to inaction, and even the maintenance of the body would not be possible for you through inaction.

lessons.text: Work as a contribution, not just for gain.
lessons.rationale: Work done for sacrifice frees you; all other action binds. Act for that sake alone, free of attachment.
lessons.old_text: Perform every action as an offering, not for gain.

verses.text: The world is bound by actions other than those performed for the sake of sacrifice; do thou, therefore, O son of Kunti (Arjuna), perform actions for that sake alone, free from attachment.

lessons.text: Give back to whatever sustains you.
lessons.rationale: Mutual nourishment sustains creation; one who enjoys the gods' gifts without offering back is a thief, and who cooks for himself alone consumes sin.
lessons.old_text: Repay what sustains you; take nothing for free.

verses.text: The Creator, having in the beginning created mankind together with sacrifice, said, "By this shall you propagate; let this be the milch cow of your desires—the cow that yields all the desired objects."
With this, nourish the gods, and may the gods nourish you; thus, nourishing each other, you shall attain the highest good.
The gods, nourished by the sacrifice, will give you the desired objects. So, he who enjoys the objects given by the gods without offering anything in return is indeed a thief.
The righteous who eat the remnants of the sacrifice are freed from all sins; but those sinful ones who cook food solely for their own sake indeed consume sin.

lessons.text: Keep giving back, or your life runs empty.
lessons.rationale: Food, rain, sacrifice, and action form one turning wheel rooted in Brahma; whoever refuses to keep it moving lives pointlessly.
lessons.old_text: Keep the wheel of giving turning, or live in vain.

verses.text: From food come forth beings; from rain, food is produced; from sacrifice arises rain, and sacrifice is born of action.
Know that action comes from Brahma, and Brahma comes from the Imperishable. Therefore, the all-pervasive Brahma ever rests in sacrifice.
He who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion, who is of sinful life, rejoicing in the senses, lives in vain, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: When you're whole within, you need nothing to prove.
lessons.rationale: One who rejoices and rests wholly in the Self has nothing to gain from deeds and depends on no being.
lessons.old_text: The self-fulfilled need nothing from action or others.

verses.text: But for that man who rejoices only in the Self, who is satisfied with the Self and is content in the Self alone, indeed there is nothing to do.
For him, there is no interest whatsoever in what is done or not done; nor does he depend on any being for any purpose.

lessons.text: Do what must be done without clinging.
lessons.rationale: Always do what must be done detached; by unattached action one attains the Supreme.
lessons.old_text: Work without attachment, and you reach the Supreme.

verses.text: Therefore, without attachment, always perform the actions that should be done; for by performing actions without attachment, one reaches the Supreme.

lessons.text: People copy what their leaders actually do.
lessons.rationale: Janaka reached perfection through action; whatever a great person does and establishes, the world imitates.
lessons.old_text: Set the standard; the world follows its leaders.

verses.text: Janaka and others attained perfection indeed through action alone; even with the intention of protecting the masses, you should perform action.
Whatever a great man does, others also do; whatever he establishes as the standard, the world follows.

lessons.text: Don't quit just because you've nothing left to gain.
lessons.rationale: Krishna has nothing left to attain yet acts, because if he stopped, people would falter and the worlds would fall into ruin.
lessons.old_text: Keep working though you need nothing, for the world's sake.

verses.text: There is nothing in the three worlds, O Arjuna, that needs to be done by Me, nor is there anything unattained that needs to be attained; yet I engage Myself in action.
For, should I not ever engage myself in action, unwearied, people would in every way follow my path, O Arjuna.
These worlds would perish if I did not perform action; I would be the author of confusion of castes and destruction of these beings.

lessons.text: Do good calmly, without unsettling others.
lessons.rationale: The wise act unattached wishing the world's welfare, and must not disturb the minds of those still attached to action.
lessons.old_text: Work detached for others' good, without unsettling them.

verses.text: As the ignorant act out of attachment to action, O Bharata, so should the wise act without attachment, wishing for the welfare of the world.
Let no wise man unsettle the minds of ignorant people who are attached to action; he should engage them in all actions, himself fulfilling them with devotion.

lessons.text: Ease up — you're not the only force at work.
lessons.rationale: Egoism alone claims doership; one who knows the gunas merely move among gunas stays unattached.
lessons.old_text: Nature does the work; drop 'I am the doer.'

verses.text: All actions are wrought in all cases by the qualities of Nature alone. He whose mind is deluded by egoism thinks, "I am the doer."

But he who knows the Truth, O mighty-armed Arjuna, about the divisions of the qualities and their functions, knowing that the Gunas, as senses, move amidst the Gunas, as the sense-objects, is not attached.
Those deluded by the qualities of Nature are attached to the functions of the qualities. The man of perfect knowledge should not unsettle the foolish one who is of imperfect knowledge.

lessons.text: Act freely, without ego or feverish worry.
lessons.rationale: Surrender every act to the Divine, mind fixed on the Self, free of hope, ego, and mental fever.
lessons.old_text: Offer all your actions to the Divine and fight.

verses.text: Renouncing all actions in Me, with the mind centered on the Self, free from hope and egoism, and from mental fever, fight thou.

lessons.text: Follow good guidance with trust, not cynicism.
lessons.rationale: Those who follow it with faith are freed from bondage; those who criticize and ignore it are lost.
lessons.old_text: Practice this teaching with faith, never scorn.

verses.text: Those who constantly practice this teaching of Mine with faith and without caviling, they too are freed from actions.
But those who criticize My teaching and do not practice it, deprived of all knowledge and lacking discernment, know them to be doomed to destruction.

lessons.text: Don't let likes and dislikes run your choices.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise follow their nature; attachment and aversion seated in the senses are enemies whose sway you must refuse.
lessons.old_text: Never let attraction and aversion rule you.

verses.text: Even a wise man acts in accordance with his own nature; beings will follow their nature; what can restraint do?
Attachment and aversion for the objects of the senses abide in the senses; let no one come under their sway; for, they are his enemies.

lessons.text: Better your own path flawed than another's perfect.
lessons.rationale: One's own duty, even flawed, surpasses another's done perfectly; another's path is fraught with fear.
lessons.old_text: Do your own duty poorly rather than another's well.

verses.text: Better is one's own duty, though devoid of merit, than the duty of another well discharged. Better is death in one's own duty; the duty of another is fraught with fear.

lessons.text: Desire and anger are your real enemies.
lessons.rationale: What drives a person to sin against his will is desire and anger, born of rajas, all-devouring foes.
lessons.old_text: Know desire and anger as your real enemy.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "But what compels man to commit sin, even against his wishes, O Varshneya (Krishna), as if constrained by force?"
The Blessed Lord said, "It is desire and it is anger, both of the quality of Rajas, all-devouring and all-sinful; know this as the foe here in this world."

lessons.text: Craving clouds judgment like smoke hides fire.
lessons.rationale: Insatiable desire, seated in senses, mind, and intellect, envelops wisdom as smoke covers fire and dust a mirror.
lessons.old_text: Desire veils your wisdom like smoke hides fire.

verses.text: As fire is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror is covered by dust, and as an embryo is surrounded by the amniotic sac, so is this enveloped by that.
O Arjuna, wisdom is enveloped by this constant enemy of the wise, in the form of desire, which is insatiable like fire.
The senses, the mind, and the intellect are said to be its seat; through these, it deludes the embodied one, veiling their wisdom.

lessons.text: First control the impulse, then uproot the craving.
lessons.rationale: Control the senses first, then destroy desire; knowing the Self is highest, restrain the self by the Self and conquer this foe.
lessons.old_text: Master your senses, then slay desire by the Self.

verses.text: Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, control your senses first and then kill this sinful thing, which destroys knowledge and realization.
They say that the senses are superior to the body; the mind is superior to the senses; the intellect is superior to the mind; and He (the Self) is superior even to the intellect.
Thus, knowing Him who is superior to the intellect and restraining the self by the Self, slay thou, O mighty-armed Arjuna, the enemy in the form of desire, hard to conquer.

skipped.reason: Narrative: Arjuna questions why Krishna urges terrible action if knowledge is superior.
Narrative: Arjuna asks for one certain path to bliss; no distinct teaching.
Expository framing restating the twofold path of knowledge and action; covered practically by 3:4-3:9.

lessons.text: Stand on the wisdom of those before you.
lessons.rationale: The yoga passed from teacher to student in unbroken succession, revived when lost.
lessons.old_text: Receive sacred wisdom handed down through teachers.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "I taught this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan; he then told it to Manu; Manu proclaimed it to Ikshvaku.
This, handed down in regular succession by the royal sages, was known. This Yoga, however, has been lost here over time, O Parantapa (burner of the foes).
That same ancient yoga has been today taught to you by me, for you are my devotee and my friend; it is the supreme secret.

lessons.text: Step up to set things right when they slip.
lessons.rationale: The divine descends age after age to protect the good and renew righteousness.
lessons.old_text: Restore what is right whenever it declines.

verses.text: Whenever there is a decline of righteousness and an increase of unrighteousness, O Arjuna, then I manifest Myself.
For the protection of the good, for the destruction of the wicked, and for the establishment of righteousness, I am born in every age.

lessons.text: See clearly, and you stop repeating the same mistakes.
lessons.rationale: Seeing the divine birth and actions in their true light frees one from returning.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine truly and end rebirth.

verses.text: He who thus knows, in their true light, My divine birth and actions, having abandoned the body, is not born again; he comes to Me, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Let understanding dissolve your fear and anger.
lessons.rationale: Those purified by the fire of knowledge and freed of these three attain the divine.
lessons.old_text: Burn attachment, fear, and anger in knowledge.

verses.text: Freed from attachment, fear, and anger, absorbed in Me, taking refuge in Me, purified by the fire of knowledge, many have attained My Being.

lessons.text: People meet you the way you approach them.
lessons.rationale: In whatever way people approach the divine, so are they answered.
lessons.old_text: Others meet you the way you approach them.

verses.text: In whatever way men approach Me, even so do I reward them; My path do men tread in all ways, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: If you want results, act—don't just wish.
lessons.rationale: Those who long for results act and sacrifice, for success comes quickly through action.
lessons.old_text: Take decisive action when you seek worldly success.

verses.text: Those who long for success in action in this world sacrifice to the gods; for success is quickly attained by men through action.

lessons.text: Do the work without letting it define you.
lessons.rationale: The Self is the non-doer, untainted and free of desire for the fruits of action.
lessons.old_text: Work knowing action never taints the Self.

verses.text: The fourfold caste has been created by Me according to the differentiation of Guna and Karma; though I am the author of it, know Me as non-doer and immutable.
Actions do not taint Me, nor do I have a desire for the fruit of actions. He who knows Me thus is not bound by actions.

lessons.text: Know when to act and when to hold back.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise are confused about action and inaction; understanding them liberates.
lessons.old_text: Study action and inaction to win freedom.

verses.text: Having known this, the ancient seekers of freedom also performed action; therefore, do thou also perform action, as the ancients did in days of yore.

What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are confused about this. Therefore, I shall teach you the nature of action and inaction, by knowing which you will be liberated from the evil of Samsara, the wheel of birth and death.
For verily, the true nature of action enjoined by the scriptures should be known, as well as that of forbidden or unlawful action, and of inaction; the nature of action is hard to understand.

lessons.text: Even doing nothing is a choice you're making.
lessons.rationale: The one who perceives this is wise, a yogi amid all his doing.
lessons.old_text: See inaction in action and action in inaction.

verses.text: He who sees inaction in action and action in inaction, he is wise among men; he is a yogi and performer of all actions.

lessons.text: Act from clarity, not from craving.
lessons.rationale: The sage's undertakings are free of selfish desire, consumed by the fire of knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Let knowledge burn desire from your work.

verses.text: He whose undertakings are all devoid of desires and selfish purposes, and whose actions have been burned by the fire of knowledge, the wise call him a sage.

lessons.text: Act without clinging to how it turns out.
lessons.rationale: Abandoning attachment to fruits and controlling the mind, one acts yet incurs no sin.
lessons.old_text: Drop hope and craving while you act.

verses.text: Having abandoned attachment to the fruits of the action, ever content, depending on nothing, he does not do anything even while being engaged in activity.
Without hope, controlling the mind and the self, having abandoned all covetousness, and performing only bodily actions, one incurs no sin.

lessons.text: Be content with what comes; chase nothing.
lessons.rationale: Content with what comes unsought and free of opposites, one acts unbound.
lessons.old_text: Stay even-minded in success and failure.

verses.text: Content with what comes to him without effort, free from the pairs of opposites and envy, even-minded in success and failure, he acts yet is not bound.

lessons.text: Selfless work leaves no weight behind.
lessons.rationale: For the detached mind fixed in knowledge, action done as sacrifice melts away entirely.
lessons.old_text: Work for sacrifice and your karma dissolves.

verses.text: To one who is devoid of attachment, who is liberated, whose mind is established in knowledge, and who works for the sake of sacrifice (for the sake of God), the whole action is dissolved.

lessons.text: Bring your full presence to whatever you do.
lessons.rationale: Brahman is the offering, the fire, and the doer for one who sees it in all action.
lessons.old_text: See the divine present in every act.

verses.text: Brahman is the oblation; Brahman is the melted butter (ghee); by Brahman is the oblation poured into the fire of Brahman; Brahman indeed shall be attained by one who always sees Brahman in action.

lessons.text: Master your impulses instead of obeying them.
lessons.rationale: Diverse yogis sacrifice senses, objects, breath, and study in the fire of restraint.
lessons.old_text: Offer your senses and breath as sacrifice.

verses.text: Some yogis perform sacrifice to the gods alone; while others, who have realized the Self, offer the Self as sacrifice in the fire of Brahman alone.
Some again offer the organ of hearing and other senses as a sacrifice in the fire of restraint; others offer sound and other objects of the senses as a sacrifice in the fire of the senses.
Others again sacrifice all the functions of the senses and those of the breath (vital energy, or Prana) in the fire of the Yoga of self-restraint, kindled by knowledge.
Others again offer wealth, austerity, and Yoga as sacrifice, while ascetics of self-restraint and rigid vows offer the study of scriptures and knowledge as sacrifice.

Others offer as sacrifice the outgoing breath into the incoming, and the incoming into the outgoing, restraining the flow of the outgoing and the incoming breaths, solely absorbed in the restraint of the breath.
Others who regulate their diet offer life-breaths in each life-breath. All these are knowers of sacrifice, whose sins are destroyed through sacrifice.

lessons.text: Give something of yourself, or gain nothing.
lessons.rationale: This world is not for the non-sacrificer; those who share the remnants reach the eternal.
lessons.old_text: Give of yourself, for without sacrifice nothing is gained.

verses.text: Those who eat the remnants of the sacrifice, which are like nectar, go to the eternal Brahman. This world is not for the one who does not perform sacrifice; how then can they have the other, O Arjuna?

lessons.text: Nothing worthwhile comes without effort.
lessons.rationale: Recognizing all sacrifices as born of action, one is liberated.
lessons.old_text: Know every sacrifice is born of action.

verses.text: Thus, manifold sacrifices are spread out before Brahman at the face of Brahman. Know them all to be born of action, and thus knowing, you shall be liberated.

lessons.text: Understanding matters more than going through the motions.
lessons.rationale: Knowledge-sacrifice is superior to material sacrifice; every action culminates in knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Prize wisdom above ritual; all action ends in knowledge.

verses.text: Superior is wisdom-sacrifice to the sacrifice with objects, O Parantapa (scorcher of the foes). All actions in their entirety, O Arjuna, culminate in knowledge.

lessons.text: Ask, listen, and stay humble enough to learn.
lessons.rationale: The realized teach knowledge to those who prostrate, question, and serve.
lessons.old_text: Approach the wise with humility and service.

verses.text: Know that the wise who have realized the truth will instruct thee in that knowledge through long prostration, supplication, and service.

lessons.text: See yourself in others, and the walls come down.
lessons.rationale: With this knowledge you perceive all beings in yourself and in the divine.
lessons.old_text: See every being within your own Self.

verses.text: Knowing that thou shalt not, O Arjuna, again be deluded like this; and by that thou shalt see all beings in thyself and also in me.

lessons.text: Understanding can carry you past any mistake.
lessons.rationale: However sinful, one crosses over all sin by knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Cross even the worst sins by the raft of knowledge.

verses.text: Even if thou art the most sinful of all sinners, yet thou shalt surely cross over all sins by the raft of knowledge.

lessons.text: Insight loosens the grip of the past.
lessons.rationale: Like fire turning fuel to ash, the fire of knowledge consumes all actions.
lessons.old_text: Let knowledge reduce your karma to ash.

verses.text: As the blazing fire reduces fuel to ashes, O Arjuna, so does the fire of knowledge reduce all actions to ash.

lessons.text: Nothing clears you like real understanding.
lessons.rationale: There is no purifier in this world equal to knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Nothing purifies you like true knowledge.

verses.text: Verily, there is no purifier in this world like knowledge. He who is perfected in Yoga finds it within the Self in due time.

lessons.text: Commit fully, stay disciplined, and peace follows.
lessons.rationale: The faithful, devoted, and sense-controlled attain knowledge and then supreme peace.
lessons.old_text: Pair faith with restraint to gain lasting peace.

verses.text: The one who is full of faith, devoted to it, and has subdued their senses obtains this knowledge; and upon obtaining the knowledge, they attain the supreme peace immediately.

lessons.text: Endless doubt drains every joy.
lessons.rationale: The ignorant and doubting perish, finding no happiness in this world or the next.
lessons.old_text: Release doubt, which destroys your happiness.

verses.text: The ignorant, the faithless, and the doubting self go to destruction; there is neither this world nor the other, nor happiness for the doubting one.

lessons.text: Cut through your doubts and get moving.
lessons.rationale: Slay the doubt born of ignorance with self-knowledge, take refuge in yoga, and act.
lessons.old_text: Cut doubt with the sword of knowledge and rise.

verses.text: He who has renounced actions through Yoga, whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, and who is self-possessed—such a one is not bound by actions, O Arjuna.
Therefore, with the sword of knowledge (of the Self), cut asunder the doubt of the self, born of ignorance, residing in your heart, and take refuge in Yoga. Arise, O Arjuna!

skipped.reason: Narrative: Arjuna's question about the timeline of the teaching.
Narrative/theological: the divine recalls its many past births.
Theological statement about the unborn divine taking form through Maya; no actionable directive.

lessons.text: Better to engage than to withdraw.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's verdict: renunciation and karma yoga both lead to bliss, but active yoga is superior.
lessons.old_text: Choose engaged action over withdrawal; both reach the goal.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "Renunciation and the Yoga of action both lead to the highest bliss; but of the two, the Yoga of action is superior to the renunciation of action."

lessons.text: Free yourself from both craving and resentment.
lessons.rationale: The true renunciate is free from the pairs of opposites, easily liberated.
lessons.old_text: Neither crave nor hate, and bondage falls away.

verses.text: He should be known as a perpetual Sannyasi who neither hates nor desires; for, free from the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed Arjuna, he is easily freed from bondage.

lessons.text: Thinking and doing are one path, not two.
lessons.rationale: Only the childish separate Sankhya and Karma Yoga; whoever masters one gains both.
lessons.old_text: See knowledge and action as one path, not two.

verses.text: Children, not the wise, speak of knowledge and the Yoga of action, or the performance of action, as though they are distinct and different; he who is truly established in one, obtains the fruits of both.
That place which is reached by the Sankhyas or the Jnanis is also reached by the Yogis (Karma Yogis). He who sees knowledge and the performance of action (Karma Yoga) as one, sees truly.

lessons.text: Start by doing; wisdom grows from practice.
lessons.rationale: Renunciation is hard without yoga; the yoga-harmonized sage quickly reaches Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Ground yourself in action first; renunciation ripens from it.

verses.text: But, O mighty-armed Arjuna, renunciation is hard to attain without Yoga; the sage who is in harmony with Yoga quickly goes to Brahman.

lessons.text: Master yourself first, then act freely.
lessons.rationale: The self-conquered doer who sees the Self in all is not stained by action.
lessons.old_text: Purify your mind and subdue the senses; then act untainted.

verses.text: He who is devoted to the path of action, whose mind is pure, who has conquered the self, who has subdued his senses, and who realizes his Self as the Self in all beings, though acting, is not tainted.

lessons.text: Notice your reactions instead of becoming them.
lessons.rationale: The knower sees seeing, eating, breathing as sense activity, not the Self acting.
lessons.old_text: Know the senses move among objects; you do nothing.

verses.text: I do nothing at all," thus would the harmonized knower of Truth think, seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, going, sleeping, and breathing.
Speaking, letting go, seizing, opening, and closing the eyes, one should be convinced that the senses move among the sense-objects.

lessons.text: Do your part, then let results roll off you.
lessons.rationale: Actions offered to Brahman without attachment leave no taint, like water on a lotus leaf.
lessons.old_text: Offer your work to the divine and stay unstained like a lotus leaf.

verses.text: He who does actions, offering them to Brahman and abandoning attachment, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not tainted by water.

lessons.text: Work to grow, not just to gain.
lessons.rationale: Yogis act without attachment through body, mind, and senses for self-purification.
lessons.old_text: Work with body and mind to purify yourself, not to gain.

verses.text: Yogis, having abandoned attachment, perform actions only through the body, mind, intellect, and even the senses, for the purification of the self.

lessons.text: Let go of the payoff and find peace.
lessons.rationale: Abandoning the fruit of action brings peace; desire for it brings bondage.
lessons.old_text: Release the fruits and rest in lasting peace.

verses.text: The one who is united (the well-poised or harmonized) having abandoned the fruit of action attains eternal peace; whereas the one who is not united (the unsteady or unbalanced), impelled by desire and attached to the fruit, is bound.

lessons.text: Stay inwardly still even while you act.
lessons.rationale: Mentally renouncing all action, the self-controlled one rests happily in the body, neither acting nor causing action.
lessons.old_text: Renounce action in the mind and dwell content within.

verses.text: Mentally renouncing all actions and being self-controlled, the embodied one happily rests in the nine-gated city, neither acting nor causing others (body and senses) to act.

lessons.text: Don't take all the credit or all the blame.
lessons.rationale: The Lord creates no agency or action; nature acts, and ignorance alone deludes beings into taking blame or credit.
lessons.old_text: Attribute doing to nature; the Self is no agent.

verses.text: Neither does the Lord create agency nor actions for the world, nor union with the fruits of actions; rather, it is Nature that acts.
The Lord takes neither the demerit nor the merit of any; knowledge is enveloped by ignorance, and beings are deluded.

lessons.text: Understanding clears confusion like sunrise.
lessons.rationale: Knowledge destroys ignorance and reveals the Supreme, as the sun reveals what darkness hid.
lessons.old_text: Let self-knowledge burn away ignorance like the sun.

verses.text: But to those whose ignorance is destroyed by knowledge of the Self, like the sun, knowledge reveals the Supreme Brahman.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully to what matters most.
lessons.rationale: Intellect, self, and goal fixed on Brahman lead to the state of no return, sins dispelled by knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Absorb your whole being in the Supreme and never return.

verses.text: Their intellect absorbed in That, their self being That, established in That, with That as their supreme goal, they go whence there is no return, their sins dispelled by knowledge.

lessons.text: Treat everyone as equally worthy.
lessons.rationale: Sages see the same Self in scholar, cow, elephant, dog, and outcaste alike.
lessons.old_text: Look with an equal eye on every being.

verses.text: Sages look with an equal eye on a Brahmana endowed with learning and humility, on a cow, an elephant, a dog, and even an outcaste.

lessons.text: Don't swing with every high and low.
lessons.rationale: The knower of Brahman neither rejoices at the pleasant nor grieves at the unpleasant.
lessons.old_text: Stay steady, unmoved by the pleasant or the unpleasant.

verses.text: Resting in Brahman, with a steady intellect and undeluded, the knower of Brahman neither rejoices upon obtaining what is pleasant nor grieves upon obtaining what is unpleasant.

lessons.text: Find joy within, not in things outside you.
lessons.rationale: Unattached to external contact, one finds endless happiness in the Self.
lessons.old_text: Find your joy in the Self, not in outer contact.

verses.text: With the self unattached to external contacts, he finds happiness in the Self; with the self engaged in the meditation of Brahman, he attains endless happiness.

lessons.text: Chasing quick pleasures ends in pain.
lessons.rationale: Enjoyments born of contact have a beginning and end and are wombs of pain; the wise avoid them.
lessons.old_text: Shun fleeting pleasures; they begin and end in pain.

verses.text: The enjoyments that arise from contact are only sources of pain, for they have a beginning and an end, O Arjuna; the wise do not rejoice in them.

lessons.text: Ride out desire and anger before they rule you.
lessons.rationale: Withstanding the impulse of desire and anger makes a happy yogi; happy and illumined within, he attains Moksha.
lessons.old_text: Conquer the surge of desire and anger to find joy within.

verses.text: He who is able, while still here in this world, to withstand the impulse born out of desire and anger before the liberation from the body, he is a Yogi, and he is a happy man.
He who is happy within, who rejoices within, and who is illuminated within, that Yogi attains absolute freedom, or Moksha, becoming Brahman himself.

lessons.text: Work for the good of others.
lessons.rationale: Sages gain absolute freedom being self-controlled and intent on the good of all beings.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself to the welfare of all beings.

verses.text: The sages obtain absolute freedom or Moksha when their sins have been destroyed, their dualities have been torn asunder, they are self-controlled, and they are intent on the welfare of all beings.

lessons.text: A quiet mind sets you free.
lessons.rationale: Freedom surrounds the self-controlled who have mastered their thoughts and realized the Self.
lessons.old_text: Still your thoughts, and freedom opens on every side.

verses.text: Absolute freedom exists on all sides for those self-controlled ascetics who are free from desire and anger, who have controlled their thoughts, and who have realized the Self.

lessons.text: Steady your breath to steady your focus.
lessons.rationale: The meditation technique: shut out contacts, fix the gaze between the brows, and balance the breath.
lessons.old_text: Steady your gaze and breath to turn attention inward.

verses.text: Shutting out all external contacts and fixing the gaze between the eyebrows, realizing the outgoing and incoming breaths moving within the nostrils.

lessons.text: Govern your mind, and fear loses its grip.
lessons.rationale: With senses and mind ever controlled and free from desire, fear, and anger, the sage is liberated.
lessons.old_text: Control senses, mind, and intellect, and be forever free.

verses.text: With the senses, mind, and intellect ever controlled, having liberation as their supreme goal, free from desire, fear, and anger, the sage is truly liberated forever.

lessons.text: See the world as friendly, and rest easier.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the Lord as enjoyer of offerings and friend of all beings brings peace.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine as friend of all, and find peace.

verses.text: He who knows Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, and the friend of all beings, attains peace.

skipped.reason: Narrative framing: Arjuna's question asking which is better, renunciation or yoga; no standalone teaching.

lessons.text: Do what's yours to do, results aside.
lessons.rationale: Defines the true renouncer as one who acts without depending on fruits, not one who merely abstains.
lessons.old_text: Do your duty without clinging to results.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said: He who performs his bounden duty without depending on the fruits of his actions—he is a sannyasi and a yogi, not he who is without fire and without action.

lessons.text: Drop the selfish motive, not the work.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is renunciation of self-centered thoughts and attachment, reached through action then settling into inaction.
lessons.old_text: Renounce selfish intentions, not action itself.

verses.text: Do you, O Arjuna, know that Yoga is what they call renunciation; no one indeed becomes a Yogi who has not renounced their thoughts.
For a sage who wishes to attain to Yoga, action is said to be the means; for the same sage who has attained Yoga, inaction is said to be the means.
When a person is not attached to the sense-objects or to actions, having renounced all thoughts, then they are said to have attained Yoga.

lessons.text: Lift yourself up; don't let yourself sink.
lessons.rationale: Raise yourself by your own self; do not let yourself sink.
lessons.old_text: Lift yourself by your own effort.

verses.text: One should raise oneself by one's own self alone; let not one lower oneself; for the self alone is one's own friend, and the self alone is one's own enemy.

lessons.text: Be your own ally, not your own worst critic.
lessons.rationale: The conquered self befriends you; the unconquered self acts as your foe.
lessons.old_text: Be your own friend, not your own enemy.

verses.text: The Self is the friend of the self of him by whom the Self has been conquered; but to the unconquered self, this Self stands in the position of an enemy, like an external foe.

lessons.text: Stay level through praise and blame alike.
lessons.rationale: The self-controlled remain even in pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.
lessons.old_text: Stay balanced through cold and heat, praise and blame.

verses.text: The Supreme Self of him who is self-controlled and peaceful remains balanced in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, as well as in honor and dishonor.

lessons.text: Gold or dirt—don't let either move you.
lessons.rationale: The content yogi treats a clod, a stone, and gold as the same.
lessons.old_text: Value wisdom over gold and stone alike.

verses.text: The Yogi who is satisfied with the knowledge and wisdom of the Self, who has conquered the senses, and to whom a clod of earth, a piece of stone, and gold are all the same, is said to have attained Nirvikalpa Samadhi.

lessons.text: Meet friend and rival with the same calm.
lessons.rationale: Excellence is being even-minded toward the loving, the hostile, and the indifferent.
lessons.old_text: Look on friend and foe with the same eye.

verses.text: He who is of the same mind towards the good-hearted, friends, enemies, the indifferent, the neutral, the hateful, the relatives, the righteous, and the unrighteous, excels.

lessons.text: Spend quiet time alone, free of wanting.
lessons.rationale: Strive constantly to steady the mind, alone, with body and mind controlled.
lessons.old_text: Practice in solitude, free from hope and greed.

verses.text: Let the yogi constantly strive to keep the mind steady, remaining in solitude, alone, with the body and mind controlled, and free from hope and greed.

lessons.text: Set up a simple, steady space to focus.
lessons.rationale: Practical instruction on preparing a stable place to sit.
lessons.old_text: Set a firm, clean seat, neither high nor low.

verses.text: In a clean spot, having established a firm seat of his own, neither too high nor too low, made of cloth, skin, and kusha grass layered one over the other.

lessons.text: Focus on one thing to clear your mind.
lessons.rationale: Seated, gather the mind and senses to a single point for inner purification.
lessons.old_text: Make the mind one-pointed to purify yourself.

verses.text: There, having made the mind one-pointed, with the actions of the mind and senses controlled, let him, seated on the seat, practice Yoga for the purification of the self.

lessons.text: Sit tall and still to settle the mind.
lessons.rationale: Concrete posture instruction: erect, steady, gaze unwandering.
lessons.old_text: Hold your body, head, and neck straight and still.

verses.text: Let him firmly hold his body, head, and neck erect and still, gazing at the tip of his nose without looking around.

lessons.text: Sit calm and fearless, focused on your aim.
lessons.rationale: Calm, fearless, disciplined, with the highest as the supreme aim.
lessons.old_text: Sit serene and fearless, mind fixed on the goal.

verses.text: Serene-minded, fearless, firm in the vow of a Brahmachari, having controlled their mind, thinking of Me and balanced in mind, let them sit, having Me as their supreme goal.

lessons.text: A steady mind brings lasting calm.
lessons.rationale: Keeping the mind balanced culminates in the peace of liberation.
lessons.old_text: A steadied mind leads you to lasting peace.

verses.text: Thus, always keeping the mind balanced, the yogi, with the mind controlled, attains the peace abiding in Me, culminating in liberation.

lessons.text: Eat, sleep, and work in moderation.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is impossible with too much or too little food and sleep; moderation destroys pain.
lessons.old_text: Eat and sleep in moderation, never in extremes.

verses.text: Verily, Yoga is not possible for him who eats too much, nor for him who does not eat at all, nor for him who sleeps too much, nor for him who is always awake, O Arjuna.
Yoga becomes the destroyer of pain for him who is moderate in eating and recreation (such as walking, etc.), who exercises moderation in action, and who is moderate in sleep and wakefulness.

lessons.text: Let the mind rest, free of wanting.
lessons.rationale: Union is the controlled mind resting in the Self, free from all longing.
lessons.old_text: Rest the mind in the Self, free of craving.

verses.text: When the perfectly controlled mind rests in the Self alone, free from longing for any of the objects of desire, then it is said, 'He is united'.

lessons.text: Keep your focus steady as a sheltered flame.
lessons.rationale: The steady yogi's mind is like a flame where no wind blows.
lessons.old_text: Keep your mind unflickering like a sheltered lamp.

verses.text: As a lamp placed in a windless spot does not flicker, so is the Yogi of a controlled mind, who practices Yoga in the Self, compared.

lessons.text: Contentment comes from knowing yourself.
lessons.rationale: When the mind quiets, one sees the Self by the Self and is satisfied.
lessons.old_text: Grow content by seeing the Self within yourself.

verses.text: When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, attains quietude, and when one sees the Self by the Self, they are satisfied in their own Self.

lessons.text: Find a joy the senses can't give or take.
lessons.rationale: Infinite bliss grasped by the intellect, transcending the senses, holds one steady.
lessons.old_text: Anchor yourself in bliss beyond the senses.

verses.text: When he (the Yogi) feels that infinite bliss which can be grasped by the pure intellect and which transcends the senses, and is established therein, never moving away from the reality.

lessons.text: Find inner steadiness, and grief can't topple you.
lessons.rationale: Established in this gain, one is unmoved even by heavy grief.
lessons.old_text: Once you find it, no sorrow can shake you.

verses.text: Having obtained it, he thinks there is no other gain superior to it; established in it, he is not moved even by heavy sorrow.

lessons.text: Practice with resolve and without despair.
lessons.rationale: Yoga, the severance from pain, is to be practiced with determination and without despair.
lessons.old_text: Practice with resolve and an undespairing mind.

verses.text: Let this be known by the name of Yoga, the severance from union with pain. This Yoga should be practiced with determination and with an undespairing mind.

lessons.text: Drop the wants your imagination invents.
lessons.rationale: Abandon all desires from mental fancy and restrain the senses from every side.
lessons.old_text: Let go of every desire born of imagination.

verses.text: Abandoning unreservedly all desires born of Sankalpa (thought and imagination) and completely restraining the whole group of senses by the mind from all sides.

lessons.text: Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.
lessons.rationale: Little by little, steady the intellect and fix the mind on the Self alone.
lessons.old_text: Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.

verses.text: Little by little, let him attain steadiness of the intellect by holding it firmly; having made the mind establish itself in the Self, let him not think of anything else.

lessons.text: When your mind wanders, gently bring it back.
lessons.rationale: From wherever the restless mind strays, draw it back under the Self's control.
lessons.old_text: Whenever the mind wanders, gently bring it back.

verses.text: From whatever cause the restless and unsteady mind wanders away, let him restrain it from that and bring it under the control of the Self alone.

lessons.text: A calm mind opens the way to deep joy.
lessons.rationale: The yogi of peaceful mind, free from passion, enjoys the infinite bliss of Brahman.
lessons.old_text: A calmed mind opens the door to supreme bliss.

verses.text: Supreme Bliss indeed comes to this Yogi whose mind is made peaceful, whose passion is quelled, who has become Brahman, and who is free from sin.
The yogi, always engaging the mind thus (in the practice of yoga), is freed from sins and easily enjoys the infinite bliss of contact with Brahman (the Eternal).

lessons.text: See yourself in everyone, and never feel alone.
lessons.rationale: The harmonized mind sees the Self everywhere and never feels separate.
lessons.old_text: See yourself in all beings and all beings in you.

verses.text: With the mind harmonized by Yoga, he sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self; he sees the same everywhere.
He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, never becomes separated from Me, nor do I from him.

lessons.text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.
lessons.rationale: The highest yogi sees all through the likeness of the Self, in pleasure and pain alike.
lessons.old_text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.

verses.text: He who, through the likeness of the Self, O Arjuna, sees reality everywhere, be it pleasure or pain, is regarded as the highest Yogi.

lessons.text: Admit it: the mind is restless and hard to hold.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna names the mind restless, turbulent, and as hard to control as the wind.
lessons.old_text: Admit the mind is restless and hard to hold.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "O Krishna, I do not see how this Yoga of equanimity, which you have taught me, can be maintained steadily, due to the restlessness of the mind."
The mind is indeed restless, turbulent, strong, and unyielding, O Krishna; I consider it as difficult to control as controlling the wind.

lessons.text: Tame a restless mind with practice and letting go.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's answer: the difficult mind is restrained by practice and dispassion.
lessons.old_text: Tame the restless mind through practice and detachment.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed Arjuna, the mind is difficult to control and restless; but with practice and dispassion, it can be restrained."

lessons.text: Self-control makes the impossible reachable.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is hard for the uncontrolled but reachable by the striving, self-controlled one.
lessons.old_text: Self-mastery makes the seemingly impossible attainable.

verses.text: I think Yoga is hard to be attained by one with an uncontrolled self, but the self-controlled and striving one can attain it by the appropriate means.

lessons.text: No honest effort is ever wasted.
lessons.rationale: The one who falls from yoga does not perish; none who does good comes to grief.
lessons.old_text: No sincere effort toward good is ever wasted.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "He who is unable to control himself, even though he has faith, and whose mind wanders away from Yoga, what end does he meet, having failed to attain perfection in Yoga, O Krishna?"
Fallen from both, does he not perish like a rent cloud, supportless, O mighty-armed one, deluded on the path of Brahman?
O Krishna, please completely dispel this doubt of mine, for it is not possible for anyone but You to do so.
The Blessed Lord said, "O Arjuna, neither in this world nor in the next will there be destruction for him; none, indeed, who does good, O my son, ever comes to grief."

lessons.text: What you build now carries into what's next.
lessons.rationale: The fallen yogi is reborn favorably and reconnects with former practice, striving further.
lessons.old_text: Every good beginning carries forward into your future.

verses.text: Having attained to the worlds of the righteous and having dwelt there for everlasting years, he who fell from Yoga is born in a house of the pure and wealthy.
Or he is born in a family of even the wisest of yogis; verily, such a birth is very difficult to obtain in this world.
Then he comes into contact with the knowledge acquired in his former body and strives even more for perfection, O Arjuna.
By that same former practice, he is borne on in spite of himself. Even he who merely wishes to know Yoga goes beyond the Brahmanic word.

lessons.text: Keep going; progress compounds over time.
lessons.rationale: The yogi who strives on, perfected gradually, reaches the highest goal.
lessons.old_text: Persist across setbacks; progress compounds over time.

verses.text: But the Yogi who strives assiduously, purified of sins and perfected gradually over many births, reaches the highest goal.

lessons.text: Steady practice beats just knowing about it.
lessons.rationale: The yogi surpasses ascetics, scholars, and ritualists; devoted practice with faith is highest.
lessons.old_text: Choose disciplined practice over mere knowledge or ritual.

verses.text: The yogi is thought to be superior to the ascetics, even superior to those who have knowledge obtained through the study of scriptures; he is also superior to men of action; therefore, be thou a yogi, O Arjuna.
And among all the Yogis, he who, full of faith and with his inner self merged in Me, worships Me is deemed by Me to be the most devoted.

lessons.text: Don't just learn it. Live it.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-2 promise knowledge combined with realization, after which nothing remains to be known.
lessons.old_text: Pair knowledge with lived realization, not theory.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Arjuna, hear how you shall, without doubt, know Me fully, with your mind intent on Me, practicing Yoga and taking refuge in Me."
I will declare to you in full this knowledge combined with realization, after knowing which nothing else remains to be known here.

lessons.text: Real understanding is rare. Earn it patiently.
lessons.rationale: Among thousands who strive, only one truly knows the essence.
lessons.old_text: Accept that true realization is rare and hard-won.

verses.text: Among thousands of men, one may perchance strive for perfection; even among those successful strivers, only one may perchance know Me in essence.

lessons.text: You are more than your body and thoughts.
lessons.rationale: The eightfold material nature is inferior; the life-element is the higher nature that upholds the world.
lessons.old_text: Distinguish your body and mind from your living essence.

verses.text: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and egoism—thus is My Nature divided eightfold.
O mighty-armed Arjuna, this is the inferior Prakriti; know it as distinct from My higher Prakriti, the very life-element, by which this world is upheld.

lessons.text: Everything is connected. Nothing stands alone.
lessons.rationale: All beings arise from the two natures; all things are strung on the divine like gems on a thread.
lessons.old_text: Trace every beginning and ending to one source.

verses.text: Know that these two are the womb of all beings; thus, I am the source and dissolution of the whole universe.
There is nothing higher than Me, O Arjuna. All this is strung on Me, like clusters of gems on a string.

lessons.text: Notice the wonder in ordinary things.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the flavor in water, light in sun and moon, fragrance in earth, life in all beings.
lessons.old_text: Notice the divine in taste, light, and sound.

verses.text: I am the flavor in water, O Arjuna; I am the light in the moon and the sun; I am the syllable Om in all the Vedas, sound in the ether and virility in men.
I am the sweet fragrance in the earth and the brilliance in the fire, the life in all beings, and I am the austerity of ascetics.

lessons.text: Behind every talent is the same quiet spark.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the seed of all beings, intelligence of the intelligent, strength free of craving, desire aligned with dharma.
lessons.old_text: See the same essence behind every excellence.

verses.text: Know Me, O Arjuna, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent, and the splendour of the splendid objects.
Of the strong, I am the strength devoid of desire and attachment, and in all beings, I am the desire in accordance with Dharma, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Don't let a passing mood distort what's real.
lessons.rationale: The world is deluded by the three gunas; this divine illusion is crossed only by taking refuge.
lessons.old_text: See past the three moods that veil reality.

verses.text: Deluded by these Natures, composed of the three qualities of Nature, all this world does not know Me as distinct from them and immutable.
Verily, this divine illusion of Mine, composed of the three qualities, is difficult to cross over; those who take refuge in Me alone, can cross over this illusion.

lessons.text: When judgment clouds, good people lose their way.
lessons.rationale: Those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow lower ways and do not seek the divine.
lessons.old_text: Watch how delusion turns people from the good.

verses.text: The evil-doers and the deluded, who are the lowest of men, do not seek Me; those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow the ways of demons.

lessons.text: Seek meaning, not just relief or reward.
lessons.rationale: Four kinds seek the divine: the distressed, the curious, the wealth-seeker, and the wise.
lessons.old_text: Let your worship rise above distress and gain.

verses.text: Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me, O Arjuna, and they are the distressed, the seekers of knowledge, the seekers of wealth, and the wise, O Lord of the Bharatas.

lessons.text: Stay steady on what matters most.
lessons.rationale: The wise, ever steadfast and devoted to the One, are dearest and held as the divine's own Self.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself with steady, single-minded love.

verses.text: Of them, the wise who are ever steadfast and devoted to the One, excel; for I am exceedingly dear to the wise, and they are dear to Me.
Indeed, all these are noble; however, I consider the wise man as My very Self; for, he is steadfast in mind and established in Me alone as the supreme goal.

lessons.text: Look for something worthwhile in everyone you meet.
lessons.rationale: After many births the wise one realizes all this is the innermost Self; such a soul is rare.
lessons.old_text: Realize the divine dwells within everything you see.

verses.text: At the end of many births, the wise man comes to Me, realizing that all this is Vaasudeva (the innermost Self); such a great soul (Mahatma) is very hard to find.

lessons.text: Craving quietly steers you toward lesser things.
lessons.rationale: Those whose wisdom is stolen by desire turn to lesser gods, led by their own nature.
lessons.old_text: Watch how desire steers you toward lesser aims.

verses.text: Those whose wisdom has been taken away by this or that desire, go to other gods, following this or that rite, led by their own nature.

lessons.text: What you give attention to grows.
lessons.rationale: The divine makes firm whatever faith a devotee holds, and grants the outcome they seek.
lessons.old_text: Whatever you worship with faith is strengthened.

verses.text: Whatever form any devotee desires to worship with faith, I make that same faith of his firm and unflinching.
Endowed with that faith, he engages in the worship of that form and obtains his desired outcome, which is ordained by Me alone.

lessons.text: Chase what lasts, not what's quick.
lessons.rationale: The reward of small minds is finite; devotees of the highest reach the highest.
lessons.old_text: Choose lasting aims over quick, finite rewards.

verses.text: Verily, the reward (fruit) that accrues to those men of small intelligence is finite. The worshippers of the gods go to them, whereas My devotees come to Me.

lessons.text: Don't mistake the surface for the whole.
lessons.rationale: The foolish think the unmanifest is limited; the divine stays veiled by Yoga-Maya.
lessons.old_text: Do not mistake the formless for mere form.

verses.text: The foolish think of Me, the Unmanifest, as having manifestation, not knowing My higher, immutable, and most excellent nature.
I am not manifest to all, veiled as I am by the Yoga-Maya. This deluded world does not know Me, who am unborn and imperishable.

lessons.text: Stay humble. No one is ever fully known.
lessons.rationale: The divine knows all beings past, present, and future, yet no one fully knows it.
lessons.old_text: Remember the knower is never fully known.

verses.text: I know, O Arjuna, the beings of the past, the present, and the future; however, no one knows Me.

lessons.text: Don't be ruled by every like and dislike.
lessons.rationale: Beings fall into delusion through the pairs of opposites; the pure-hearted, freed from them, worship steadfastly.
lessons.old_text: Free yourself from the pull of like and dislike.

verses.text: O Bharata, all beings are subject to delusion at birth due to the delusion of the pairs of opposites arising from desire and aversion, O Parantapa.
But those men of virtuous deeds, whose sins have come to an end and who are freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites, worship Me steadfastly, with their vows.

lessons.text: Something in you stays constant through every change.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-4 answer Arjuna's questions by defining Brahman as the imperishable Supreme; the applicable teaching is to know the eternal Self behind perishable nature.
lessons.old_text: Know the imperishable Self behind all changing forms.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "What is Brahman? What is Adhyatma? What is action, O best among men? What is Adhibhuta declared to be? And, what is Adhidaiva said to be?"
Who and how is Adhiyajna here in this body, O destroyer of Madhu? And how, at the time of death, are You to be known by the self-controlled?

The Blessed Lord said, "Brahman is the Imperishable, the Supreme; its essential nature is called Self-knowledge; the offering (to the gods) that causes the existence and manifestation of beings and sustains them is called action."
Adhibhuta—knowledge of the elements—pertains to My perishable nature, and the Purusha, or the Soul, is the Adhidaiva; I alone am the Adhiyajna here in this body, O best among the embodied.

lessons.text: You become whatever you dwell on.
lessons.rationale: Both verses state that the being one dwells on at death is the state one attains.
lessons.old_text: Whatever you remember at the end, you become.

verses.text: And whoever, leaving their body, goes forth remembering Me alone at the time of death, they will attain My Being; there is no doubt about this.
Whoever at the end leaves the body, thinking of any being, to that being only does he go, O son of Kunti (Arjuna), due to his constant thought of that being.

lessons.text: Keep what matters in mind while you work.
lessons.rationale: Krishna tells Arjuna to remember Him at all times and still fight — devotion woven through daily action.
lessons.old_text: Remember what matters most while you still act.

verses.text: Therefore, at all times, remember Me only and fight. With your mind and intellect fixed on Me, you will undoubtedly come to Me alone.

lessons.text: Steady the mind with daily practice.
lessons.rationale: The Supreme is reached through habitual meditation and a mind that does not wander.
lessons.old_text: Steady the mind through daily, unwavering practice.

verses.text: With the mind not moving towards any other thing, made steadfast through the practice of habitual meditation, and constantly meditating, one goes to the Supreme Person, the Resplendent, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Lift your mind toward something larger than yourself.
lessons.rationale: These verses describe meditating on the luminous, all-supporting Supreme beyond ignorance, with an unwavering devoted mind.
lessons.old_text: Contemplate what is vast, ancient, and beyond darkness.

verses.text: Whosoever meditates on the Omniscient, the Ancient, the Ruler of the whole world, minuter than an atom, the supporter of all, of inconceivable form, effulgent like the sun and beyond the darkness of ignorance.
At the time of death, with an unwavering mind, endowed with devotion, by the power of Yoga, fixing the whole life-breath in the middle of the two eyebrows, he reaches that resplendent Supreme Person.

lessons.text: Quiet the noise and turn inward.
lessons.rationale: Withdraw from external gates and confine the mind within to practice concentration.
lessons.old_text: Close the senses and gather your mind inward.

verses.text: That which is declared to be Imperishable by those who know the Vedas, that which the self-controlled (ascetics or Sannyasins) and passion-free enter, that goal, desiring which celibacy is practised, I will declare to thee in brief.
Having closed all the gates, confined the mind in the heart, and fixed the life-breath in the head, engage in the practice of concentration.

lessons.text: Anchor a restless mind on one word.
lessons.rationale: Uttering the single syllable Om steadies the departing mind toward the Supreme goal.
lessons.old_text: Anchor your restless mind to a single word.

verses.text: Uttering the one-syllabled Om, the Brahman, and remembering Me, he who departs, leaving the body, attains the Supreme Goal.

lessons.text: Practiced daily, the hard thing grows easy.
lessons.rationale: The one who remembers single-mindedly and daily finds the Supreme easily attainable.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself constantly and the way grows easy.

verses.text: I am easily attainable by that ever-steadfast yogi who constantly and daily remembers me for a long time, not thinking of anything else with a single-minded or one-pointed focus, O Partha.

lessons.text: Chase the lasting and step off the treadmill.
lessons.rationale: Those who attain the Supreme escape rebirth into this impermanent place of pain; even Brahma's world returns.
lessons.old_text: Reach the lasting, and leave suffering's cycle behind.

verses.text: Having attained Me, these great souls do not take birth again here—a place of pain and impermanence—but have reached the highest perfection of liberation.
All the worlds, including the world of Brahma, are subject to return again, O Arjuna; but he who reaches Me, O son of Kunti, has no rebirth.

lessons.text: Everything built in time eventually dissolves.
lessons.rationale: The cosmic day-and-night cycle shows all manifest worlds and beings arise and dissolve repeatedly — the applicable lesson is the impermanence of all time-bound things.
lessons.old_text: Everything built in time will dissolve in time.

verses.text: Those who know the day of Brahma, which lasts a thousand Yugas, and the night, which also lasts a thousand Yugas, know day and night.
From the Unmanifested, all the manifested worlds proceed upon the arrival of the 'day'; upon the arrival of the 'night', they dissolve indeed into that which is known as the Unmanifested.
This same multitude of beings, being born again and again, helplessly dissolves, O Arjuna, into the Unmanifested at the coming of the night and comes forth at the coming of the day.

lessons.text: Invest in what outlasts you.
lessons.rationale: Beyond the dissolving unmanifest is an eternal one that is never destroyed — the highest, non-returning goal.
lessons.old_text: Seek the eternal that survives when all else ends.

verses.text: But verily, there exists higher than this Unmanifested, another Unmanifested Eternal, which is not destroyed even when all beings are destroyed.
What is known as the Unmanifested and the Imperishable, That is said to be the highest goal. Those who reach It do not return (to this Samsara). That is My supreme abode (place or state).

lessons.text: Give your whole heart to one aim.
lessons.rationale: The highest is reached by unswerving, single-pointed devotion to the one who pervades all.
lessons.old_text: Give your whole heart to one undivided aim.

verses.text: That highest Purusha, O Arjuna, is attainable by unswerving devotion to Him alone, within Whom all beings dwell and by Whom all this is pervaded.

lessons.text: Move toward the light, not the smoke.
lessons.rationale: The two paths (bright leading to no return, dark leading to return) are largely cosmological; the extractable teaching is to choose the path of light. Verses 24-25's solstice/fortnight enumeration is technical.
lessons.old_text: Move toward the light, not toward the smoke.

verses.text: Now I will tell you, O chief of the Bharatas, the times of departure at which the Yogis will return or not return.
Fire, light, daytime, the bright fortnight, the six months of the northern path of the sun (the northern solstice) departing, then men who know Brahman go to Brahman.
Attaining the lunar light through smoke, night time, the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southern path of the sun (the southern solstice), the yogi returns.
The bright and dark paths of the world are thought to be eternal; one leads to no return, and the other leads to return.

lessons.text: Stay steady in practice through every season.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the paths, the yogi is never deluded and remains steadfast in yoga at all times.
lessons.old_text: Stay steadfast in practice through every season.

verses.text: Knowing these paths, O Arjuna, no yogi is deluded; therefore, at all times, be steadfast in yoga.

lessons.text: Inner work outlasts every outward show.
lessons.rationale: The yogi surpasses the merits of study, sacrifice, and austerity, reaching the Supreme Abode.
lessons.old_text: Inner practice outlasts every ritual and reward.

verses.text: Whatever fruit of merit is declared (in the scriptures) to accrue from (the study of) the Vedas, (the performance of) sacrifices, (the practice of) austerities, and gifts, beyond all this goes the Yogi, having known this; and he attains to the Supreme, Primeval (first or ancient) Abode.

lessons.text: Meet new ideas without cynicism.
lessons.rationale: The secret is declared to one who does not cavil; open, uncynical receptivity is the prerequisite.
lessons.old_text: Approach sacred knowledge without cynicism or cavil.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "I shall now declare to thee, who does not cavil, the greatest secret—the knowledge combined with experience (Self-realisation). Having known this, thou shalt be free from evil."

lessons.text: The best path is often the simplest.
lessons.rationale: The royal science is called supremely pure yet very easy to perform and imperishable.
lessons.old_text: The highest path is the simplest to walk.

verses.text: This is the royal science, the royal secret, the supreme purifier, realizable by direct intuitive knowledge, according to righteousness, very easy to perform and imperishable.

lessons.text: Without conviction, you drift back to the start.
lessons.rationale: Those lacking faith return to the worldly path without attaining the divine.
lessons.old_text: Without faith, you circle back where you started.

verses.text: Those who have no faith in this Dharma, O Parantapa, return to the path of this world without attaining Me.

lessons.text: Be part of everything, attached to nothing.
lessons.rationale: All beings rest in the divine as wind rests in ether, yet it does not dwell in them; pervasion without attachment.
lessons.old_text: The divine pervades everything yet is bound by nothing.

verses.text: All of this world is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest aspect; all beings exist within Me, but I do not dwell within them.
Nor do beings exist in Me (in reality); behold, My divine Yoga, which supports all beings, but does not dwell in them, is My Self, the efficient cause of beings.
As the mighty wind, moving everywhere, always rests in the ether, so too, know that all beings rest in Me.

lessons.text: What ends makes room for a new beginning.
lessons.rationale: Beings return to nature at the end of a cycle and are sent forth again; the rhythm of dissolution and renewal.
lessons.old_text: All that arises will dissolve and rise again.

verses.text: All beings, O Arjuna, go into My Nature at the end of a Kalpa; I send them forth again at the beginning of the next Kalpa.
Animating My Nature, I again and again send forth all this multitude of beings, helpless under the force of Nature.

lessons.text: Do the work without getting tangled in it.
lessons.rationale: The divine sits indifferent and unattached, so its acts do not bind; a model for acting without entanglement.
lessons.old_text: Act while resting untouched, like a witness.

verses.text: These acts do not bind Me, O Arjuna, sitting as one indifferent, unattached to those acts.
Under Me, as supervisor, Nature produces the moving and the unmoving; therefore, O Arjuna, the world revolves.

lessons.text: Don't overlook greatness in ordinary packaging.
lessons.rationale: Fools disregard the divine clad in human form, and their hopes and deeds turn vain; a warning against shallow judgment.
lessons.old_text: Do not dismiss the sacred in ordinary form.

verses.text: Fools disregard Me, clad in human form, not knowing My higher Being as the great Lord of all beings.
They are possessed of the deceitful nature of demons and undivine beings, filled with vain hopes, vain actions, and vain knowledge that is senseless.

lessons.text: Do one thing at a time, completely.
lessons.rationale: Great souls worship with single-minded devotion, firm in their vows and constant in striving.
lessons.old_text: Worship with a single, undivided mind.

verses.text: But the great souls, O Arjuna, partaking of My divine nature, worship Me with a single-minded devotion, knowing Me as the imperishable source of all beings.
Always glorifying Me, striving, firm in their vows, prostrating themselves before Me, they worship Me with steadfast devotion.

lessons.text: The same thread runs through everyone.
lessons.rationale: The divine is named as goal, shelter, mother, father, and seed of all; worship it as one yet manifold everywhere.
lessons.old_text: See the one divine behind every form.

verses.text: Others also, sacrificing with the wisdom-sacrifice, worship Me, the All-Faced, as one, distinct, and manifold.
I am Kratu; I am Yajna; I am the offering to the manes; I am the medicinal herbs and all plants; I am the Mantra; I am also the ghee or melted butter; I am the fire; I am the oblation.

I am the father of this world, the mother, the dispenser of the fruits of actions, and the grandfather; the one thing to be known, the purifier, the sacred monosyllable (Om), and also the Rik, Sama, and Yajur Vedas.
I am the goal, the supporter, the Lord, the witness, the abode, the shelter, the friend, the origin, the dissolution, the foundation, the treasure-house, and the imperishable seed.

lessons.text: Quick rewards run out. Deeper ones don't.
lessons.rationale: Vedic ritualists win heaven but return when their merit is exhausted, caught in coming and going.
lessons.old_text: Ritual rewards run out; the divine does not.

verses.text: As the sun, I give heat; I withhold and send forth the rain; I am immortality and also death, existence and non-existence, O Arjuna.

The knowers of the three Vedas, the drinkers of Soma, purified of all sins, worshipping Me through sacrifices, pray for the way to heaven; they reach the holy world of the Lord of the gods and enjoy the divine pleasures of the gods in heaven.

They, having enjoyed the vast heaven, enter the world of mortals when their merit is exhausted; thus abiding by the injunctions of the three (Vedas) and desiring objects of desires, they attain to the state of coming and going.

lessons.text: Commit fully, and you'll find what you need.
lessons.rationale: For those who worship with undivided minds, the divine secures what they lack and preserves what they have.
lessons.old_text: Devote yourself fully, and your needs are carried.

verses.text: For those men who worship Me alone, thinking of no one else, for those ever-united, I secure what they have not already possessed and preserve what they already possess.

lessons.text: Every sincere effort counts, whatever its form.
lessons.rationale: Even worship offered to other gods reaches the one divine; worshippers go to whatever they truly seek.
lessons.old_text: Every sincere prayer reaches the same source.

verses.text: Even those devotees who, endowed with faith, worship other gods, worship Me alone, O Arjuna, but by the wrong method.
For I alone am the enjoyer and Lord of all sacrifices; but they do not know Me in reality, and thus they return to this mortal world.
The worshippers of the gods go to them; the ancestor-worshippers go to the manes; the worshippers of the deities who preside over the elements go to them; but My devotees come to Me.

lessons.text: Given sincerely, even a small gift means everything.
lessons.rationale: A leaf, flower, fruit, or water offered with devotion by a pure heart is accepted; the sincerity matters, not the size.
lessons.old_text: Even a leaf, given with love, is enough.

verses.text: Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or a little water, that, so offered devotedly by the pure-minded, I accept.

lessons.text: Pour yourself fully into whatever you do.
lessons.rationale: Whatever you do, eat, give, or practice, do it as an offering to the divine.
lessons.old_text: Offer whatever you do as devotion.

verses.text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice, whatever you give, whatever austerity you practice, O Arjuna, do it as an offering to Me.

lessons.text: Do the work, release the outcome.
lessons.rationale: Offering all deeds frees you from the bonds of good and evil fruits through the yoga of renunciation.
lessons.old_text: Offer your actions and their fruits lose their grip.

verses.text: Thus, you shall be freed from the bonds of actions yielding good and evil fruits; with the mind steadfast in the Yoga of renunciation, and liberated, you shall come to Me.

lessons.text: You get back the closeness you give.
lessons.rationale: None is hateful or dear, yet those who worship with devotion dwell in the divine and it in them.
lessons.old_text: The divine is equal to all, yet near to the devoted.

verses.text: I am the same to all beings; there is none hateful or dear to Me; but those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I am also in them.

lessons.text: It's never too late to change direction.
lessons.rationale: Even the most sinful, once rightly resolved in devotion, soon becomes righteous; the devotee never perishes.
lessons.old_text: No one who truly turns back is beyond redemption.

verses.text: Even if the most sinful worships Me, with devotion to no one else, he should indeed be regarded as righteous, for he has rightly resolved.
Soon he becomes righteous and attains eternal peace; O Arjuna, proclaim thou for certain that My devotee never perishes.

lessons.text: The best in life is open to everyone.
lessons.rationale: All who take refuge, regardless of birth or station, attain the supreme goal.
lessons.old_text: The highest goal is open to everyone.

verses.text: For, taking refuge in Me, they who, O Arjuna, may be of a sinful birth—women, Vaisyas, and Sudras—attain the Supreme Goal.
How much more easily, then, do Brahmins and devoted royal saints attain the goal? Having come to this impermanent and unhappy world, do thou worship Me.

lessons.text: Show up fully: mind, heart, and effort.
lessons.rationale: The chapter closes with total self-offering: mind, devotion, sacrifice, and reverence united toward the supreme.
lessons.old_text: Fix your mind, bow, and give yourself wholly.

verses.text: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; bow down to Me; having thus united your whole self to Me, taking Me as the supreme goal, you will come to Me.

lessons.text: See clearly, and illusion loses its grip.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-3 frame the chapter: knowing the source as unborn Lord liberates mortals from delusion and sin.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine as unborn and beginningless, and be freed from delusion.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, Again, O mighty-armed Arjuna, listen to my supreme word which I will declare to you, who are beloved, for your welfare.
Neither the hosts of the gods nor the great sages know My origin; for I am the source of all the gods and the great sages in every way.
He who knows Me as unborn and beginningless, as the great Lord of the worlds, he among mortals is undeluded and is liberated from all sins.

lessons.text: Share what moves you; it wakes others up.
lessons.rationale: The wise, rooted in the source of all, worship with delight and enlighten one another — the devotional response to divine origin.
lessons.old_text: Absorb your mind in the divine and awaken others by sharing it.

verses.text: I am the source of all; from me everything evolves; Understanding this, the wise, endowed with meditation, worship me.
With their minds and lives wholly absorbed in Me, they enlighten each other and ever speak of Me, being satisfied and delighted.

lessons.text: Show up with love, and clarity follows.
lessons.rationale: Loving, steadfast devotion earns the yoga of discrimination that leads to the divine.
lessons.old_text: Worship steadfastly with love, and clear discernment will be given.

verses.text: To those who are ever steadfast, worshipping me with love, I give the yoga of discrimination, by which they come to me.

lessons.text: Let understanding burn away your confusion.
lessons.rationale: The divine dwelling within destroys ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Let the lamp of knowledge burn away the darkness of ignorance.

verses.text: Out of mere compassion for them, I, dwelling within their selves, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.

lessons.text: The best of anything is a glimpse of something larger.
lessons.rationale: Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful is a fragment of divine splendor.
lessons.old_text: See every glory and power as a spark of the divine splendor.

verses.text: Whatever being there is glorious, prosperous, or powerful, know that to be a manifestation of a part of My splendor.
