lessons.text: Meet what overwhelms you with humility, not fear.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna, awestruck and trembling, joins his palms and bows — the human response of humility before the vast.
lessons.old_text: Bow your head before what dwarfs you.

verses.text: Then, Arjuna, filled with wonder and his hair standing on end, bowed his head to the God and spoke with palms joined.

lessons.text: Do your part; the outcome isn't yours to control.
lessons.rationale: 'I am Time, the destroyer; they are already slain — be a mere instrument.' Act your role; the result is not yours to author.
lessons.old_text: Do your part; the outcome is already set.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "I am the full-grown, world-destroying Time, now engaged in destroying the worlds. Even without you, none of the warriors arrayed in the hostile armies will live."
Therefore, stand up and obtain fame. Conquer the enemies and enjoy the unparalleled kingdom. Verily, by Me they have already been slain; be thou a mere instrument, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Face your fight with courage, not dread.
lessons.rationale: Told the outcome is decided, Arjuna is urged to act with courage, not distress: fight and you shall prevail.
lessons.old_text: Fight your battle without fear of losing.

verses.text: Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and other brave warriors have already been slain by Me; do not be distressed with fear; fight and you shall conquer your enemies in battle.

lessons.text: Honor what you've been taking for granted.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna repents for his casual, familiar treatment of Krishna at play and meals, and humbly begs forgiveness.
lessons.old_text: Apologize for the times you took greatness lightly.

verses.text: Whatever I have presumptuously said from carelessness or love, addressing You as O Krishna! O Yadava! O Friend! regarding You merely as a friend, unknowing of Your greatness.

In whatever way I may have insulted You for the sake of fun, while at play, reposing, sitting, or at meals, when alone (with You), O Krishna, or in company, that I implore You, immeasurable one, to forgive.

Thou art the Father of this world, both moving and unmoving. Thou art to be adored by this world; Thou, the greatest Guru; for none exists who is equal to Thee; how then could there be another superior to Thee in the three worlds, O Being of unrivaled power?

Therefore, bowing down and prostrating my body, I crave Thy forgiveness, O adorable Lord. As a father forgives his son, a friend his dear friend, a lover his beloved, even so may Thou forgive me, O God.

lessons.text: Wholehearted attention reveals what routine can't.
lessons.rationale: Not by Vedas, austerity, or sacrifice — but by single-minded devotion alone can the divine be truly known and seen.
lessons.old_text: Only devotion, not ritual, reveals the deepest truth.

verses.text: Neither by the Vedas, nor by austerity, nor by gift, nor by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as thou hast seen Me so easily.
But by single-minded devotion, I can be known, seen, and entered into in reality, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Work without clinging, and hold no one as enemy.
lessons.rationale: The one who acts for the Supreme, free from attachment and bearing no hatred toward any being, reaches the divine.
lessons.old_text: Work for the sacred, unattached and free of enmity.

verses.text: He who does all actions for Me, who regards Me as the Supreme, who is devoted to Me, who is free from attachment, who bears no enmity towards any creature, he comes to Me, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: The concrete is easier to hold than the abstract.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna's question and Krishna's verdict: those who fix their minds on a personal, knowable divine with faith are the best in yoga.
lessons.old_text: Loving the personal divine beats worshipping the abstract.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "Which of them are better versed in Yoga—those who steadfastly worship You, or those who worship the imperishable and the unmanifested?"
The Blessed Lord said, "In My opinion, those who fix their minds on Me, worship Me ever steadfastly, and are endowed with supreme faith, are the best in Yoga."

lessons.text: The abstract road is valid, just steeper to walk.
lessons.rationale: Worshippers of the unmanifest also reach the goal by restraining senses and serving all, but their road is steeper for embodied beings.
lessons.old_text: The formless path is valid but harder to walk.

verses.text: Those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifest, the omnipresent, the unthinkable, the immovable, and the eternal.
Having restrained all the senses, being even-minded everywhere, and intent on the welfare of all beings, they verily come unto Me.
Greater is their trouble whose minds are set on the unmanifested, for the goal of the unmanifested is very hard for the embodied to reach.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully, and you'll be carried through.
lessons.rationale: Those who offer all actions and rest their minds on the divine are swiftly rescued from the ocean of samsara.
lessons.old_text: Surrender your whole self and be carried across.

verses.text: But to those who worship Me, renouncing all actions in Me, regarding Me as the supreme goal, meditating on Me with single-minded yoga.
To those whose minds are set on Me, O Arjuna, verily I soon become the savior out of the ocean of Samsara.

lessons.text: Start by giving it your full attention.
lessons.rationale: Top rung of the graded ladder: absorb mind and intellect fully in the divine and dwell there.
lessons.old_text: First, simply fix your mind on the divine.

verses.text: Fix your mind on Me, and your intellect in Me. Then you will certainly live in Me alone hereafter.

lessons.text: If focus fails, keep showing up.
lessons.rationale: Second rung: when steady focus fails, use repeated disciplined practice (abhyasa yoga) to draw near.
lessons.old_text: If you cannot hold focus, practice reaching for it.

verses.text: If you are unable to fix your mind steadily on Me, then seek to reach Me through the yoga of constant practice, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Can't stay steady? Act on it anyway.
lessons.rationale: Third rung: even without practice, dedicate your actions to the divine and you still attain perfection.
lessons.old_text: If practice is too hard, just work for the divine.

verses.text: If you are unable to practice even this Abhyasa Yoga, be intent on doing actions for My sake; even by doing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.

lessons.text: At minimum, let go of needing a reward.
lessons.rationale: Lowest rung of the fallback ladder: renounce the fruits of every action with self-control.
lessons.old_text: If all else fails, at least surrender the results.

verses.text: If you are unable to do even this, then, resort to union with Me and renounce the fruits of all actions with self-control.

lessons.text: Release the outcome and peace arrives at once.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing the fruits of action is ranked above knowledge and meditation because peace immediately follows it.
lessons.old_text: Let go of outcomes and peace arrives at once.

verses.text: Better indeed is knowledge than practice; better than knowledge is meditation; better than meditation is the renunciation of the fruits of actions: peace immediately follows renunciation.

lessons.text: Wish no one harm; be a friend to all.
lessons.rationale: Opens the devotee-qualities list: freedom from hatred, friendliness, and compassion toward every being.
lessons.old_text: Hate no creature; be a friend to all.

verses.text: He who hates no creature, is friendly and compassionate to all, is free from attachment and egoism, is balanced in pleasure and pain, and is forgiving.

lessons.text: Stay steady through highs and lows; forgive quickly.
lessons.rationale: Same verse names a distinct virtue: evenness across pleasure and pain, free of ego, and forgiving.
lessons.old_text: Stay balanced in pleasure and pain, quick to forgive.

verses.text: He who hates no creature, is friendly and compassionate to all, is free from attachment and egoism, is balanced in pleasure and pain, and is forgiving.

lessons.text: Stay content and steady, your resolve firm.
lessons.rationale: The devotee dear to the divine is ever content, steady in meditation, self-controlled, and firm in conviction.
lessons.old_text: Be content and steady, your resolve unshaken.

verses.text: Ever content, steady in meditation, self-controlled, possessing firm conviction, with the mind and intellect dedicated to Me, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.

lessons.text: Trouble no one, and let nothing trouble you.
lessons.rationale: Distinct quality: causing no agitation to others and remaining free from joy, anger, fear, and anxiety.
lessons.old_text: Neither disturb the world nor let it disturb you.

verses.text: He whom the world does not agitate, and who cannot be agitated by the world, and who is freed from joy, anger, fear, and anxiety—he is dear to Me.

lessons.text: Want less, stay clear, drop the anxious scheming.
lessons.rationale: The devotee is free from wants, pure, capable, unconcerned, and has renounced all anxious undertakings.
lessons.old_text: Want nothing, stay pure, and drop every anxious scheme.

verses.text: He who is free from wants, pure, expert, unconcerned, and free from pain, renouncing all undertakings and commencements, he who is devoted to Me is dear to Me.

lessons.text: Stop clinging and recoiling; let things be.
lessons.rationale: Neither rejoicing nor hating, grieving nor craving, the devotee renounces both good and evil in devotion.
lessons.old_text: Stop chasing and recoiling; rise above good and bad.

verses.text: He who neither rejoices nor hates, nor grieves nor desires, renouncing both good and evil, and who is full of devotion, is dear to Me.

lessons.text: Treat friend and foe, honor and insult, alike.
lessons.rationale: Equanimity toward friend and enemy, honor and dishonor, heat and cold, pleasure and pain, free of attachment.
lessons.old_text: Treat friend and foe, honor and insult, the same.

verses.text: He who is the same to foe and friend, and also in honor and dishonor, who is the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, and who is free from attachment.

lessons.text: Let praise and blame pass through you.
lessons.rationale: Closing devotee virtue: equal to censure and praise, silent, satisfied with whatever comes, steady-minded.
lessons.old_text: Take praise and blame alike; be content with anything.

verses.text: He to whom censure and praise are equal, who is silent, content with anything, homeless, of a steady mind, and full of devotion; that man is dear to me.

lessons.text: Follow through with faith, all in.
lessons.rationale: Summary verse: those who follow this immortal dharma with faith, making the divine their goal, are exceedingly dear.
lessons.old_text: Walk this path with faith and become truly beloved.

verses.text: They who follow this immortal Dharma, endowed with faith and regarding Me as their supreme goal, are exceedingly dear to Me.

lessons.text: Let desire and pain pass; they aren't you.
lessons.rationale: Lists the field's constituents and modifications, distinct from the knower.
lessons.old_text: Count desire, hatred, and pain as passing field, not self.

verses.text: The great elements, egoism, intellect, and also the Unmanifested Nature, the ten senses, and one mind, and the five objects of the senses.
Desire, hatred, pleasure, pain, the aggregate (body), intelligence, and fortitude—the field has thus been briefly described with its modifications.

lessons.text: Stay humble, honest, and free of pretense.
lessons.rationale: Mines humility, unpretentiousness, and uprightness from the knowledge list.
lessons.old_text: Stay humble, upright, and free of pretense.

verses.text: Humility, unpretentiousness, non-injury, forgiveness, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadfastness, and self-control.

lessons.text: Harm no one; forgive; keep yourself in check.
lessons.rationale: Mines non-injury, forgiveness, and self-control as a distinct virtue-cluster.
lessons.old_text: Injure no one; forgive, and hold yourself in check.

verses.text: Humility, unpretentiousness, non-injury, forgiveness, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadfastness, and self-control.

lessons.text: Stay humble enough to keep learning.
lessons.rationale: Mines service to the teacher, purity, and steadfastness.
lessons.old_text: Serve your teacher; stay pure and steadfast.

verses.text: Humility, unpretentiousness, non-injury, forgiveness, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadfastness, and self-control.

lessons.text: Loosen your grip on pleasure and ego.
lessons.rationale: Indifference to sense objects and absence of egoism.
lessons.old_text: Loosen the ego's grip on sense pleasures.

verses.text: Indifference to the objects of the senses and also absence of egoism; perceiving the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain.

lessons.text: Face aging, sickness, and death clearly.
lessons.rationale: Perceiving the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain.
lessons.old_text: Face clearly the pain in birth, sickness, and death.

verses.text: Indifference to the objects of the senses and also absence of egoism; perceiving the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain.

lessons.text: Don't tie your identity to family or home.
lessons.rationale: Non-identification of the Self with son, wife, home, and the rest.
lessons.old_text: Don't bind your identity to family or home.

verses.text: Non-attachment, non-identification of the Self with son, wife, home, and the rest, and constant even-mindedness in the face of the attainment of both desirable and undesirable.

lessons.text: Stay even whether outcomes please or disappoint.
lessons.rationale: Constant even-mindedness toward the desirable and undesirable.
lessons.old_text: Stay even whether outcomes please or disappoint.

verses.text: Non-attachment, non-identification of the Self with son, wife, home, and the rest, and constant even-mindedness in the face of the attainment of both desirable and undesirable.

lessons.text: Make room for solitude to hear yourself.
lessons.rationale: Unswerving devotion, resorting to solitude, distaste for crowds.
lessons.old_text: Withdraw into solitude to deepen your devotion.

verses.text: Unswerving devotion to Me through the Yoga of non-separation, resorting to solitary places, and a distaste for the company of people.

lessons.text: Keep coming back to knowing yourself.
lessons.rationale: Constancy in Self-knowledge is declared to be true knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Keep returning your attention to the Self.

verses.text: Constancy in Self-knowledge, the perception of the end of true knowledge—this is declared to be knowledge, and what is opposed to it is ignorance.

lessons.text: Support everything; cling to nothing.
lessons.rationale: Unattached yet supporting all; the experiencer without attachment.
lessons.old_text: Support everything while clinging to nothing.

verses.text: Shining by the functions of all the senses, yet without being attached to them; unattached, yet supporting all; devoid of qualities, yet the experiencer of them.

lessons.text: What you cling to shapes who you become.
lessons.rationale: Attachment to nature's qualities causes birth in good and evil wombs.
lessons.old_text: Notice how clinging to moods shapes your rebirth.

verses.text: The soul seated in Nature experiences the qualities born of Nature; attachment to the qualities is the cause of its birth in good and evil wombs.

lessons.text: Watch your reactions without meddling in them.
lessons.rationale: The Supreme Soul as observer, permitter, and sustainer within the body.
lessons.old_text: Let the inner witness watch and permit, not meddle.

verses.text: The Supreme Soul in this body is also called the observer, the permitter, the sustainer, the enjoyer, the great Lord, and the Supreme Self.

lessons.text: Grow through reflection, learning, or action.
lessons.rationale: The three paths by which people behold the Self.
lessons.old_text: Reach the Self by meditation, knowledge, or action.

verses.text: Some behold the Self within themselves through meditation, others through the Yoga of knowledge, and still others through the Yoga of action.

lessons.text: Harm others and you harm yourself.
lessons.rationale: Seeing the same Lord everywhere, one does not destroy the Self by the self.
lessons.old_text: Seeing one Self everywhere, you stop harming yourself.

verses.text: For he who truly sees the same Lord dwelling everywhere does not destroy the Self by the self; rather, he attains the highest goal.

lessons.text: Touch everything; let nothing stain you.
lessons.rationale: Like all-pervading ether, the Self seated in the body is not tainted.
lessons.old_text: Stay unstained like space that touches everything.

verses.text: As the all-pervading ether is not tainted, due to its subtlety, so the Self seated everywhere in the body is not tainted either.

skipped.reason: Arjuna's framing question, no teaching.
Transitional setup ('hear from Me what the field is').
Narrative note that sages have sung of it in many ways.
Recap/promise summarizing the field and knowable already taught.

lessons.text: Clear understanding sets you free.
lessons.rationale: Opening promise: those who take refuge in this supreme knowledge attain unity and are undisturbed by creation or dissolution.
lessons.old_text: Take refuge in the highest knowledge for freedom.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "I will again declare to thee that supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, having known which all the sages have gone to supreme perfection after this life."
Those who, having taken refuge in this knowledge, have attained unity with Me, are neither born at the time of creation nor disturbed at the time of dissolution.

lessons.text: Three forces drive you: clarity, craving, inertia.
lessons.rationale: Introduces sattva, rajas, tamas as qualities born of nature that bind the embodied self; the chapter's thesis.
lessons.old_text: Recognize the three inborn forces that bind you.

verses.text: These qualities, O Arjuna, born of Nature, bind fast in the body of the embodied, the indestructible: purity, passion, and inertia.

lessons.text: Even clarity and joy can quietly trap you.
lessons.rationale: Sattva-specific: luminous and pure, yet it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
lessons.old_text: Watch how even clarity and joy quietly bind.

verses.text: Of these, sattva, which is luminous and healthy due to its stainlessness, binds one by attachment to happiness and knowledge, O sinless one.

lessons.text: Craving chains you to restless action.
lessons.rationale: Rajas-specific: passion, thirst, and attachment that bind through ceaseless action.
lessons.old_text: See how craving chains you to restless action.

verses.text: Know, O Arjuna, that Rajas is of the nature of passion, the source of thirst and attachment; it binds fast the embodied one by attachment to action.

lessons.text: Catch the dullness pulling you toward neglect.
lessons.rationale: Tamas-specific: born of ignorance, it binds through heedlessness, indolence, and sleep.
lessons.old_text: Catch the dullness luring you toward sleep and neglect.

verses.text: But know thou Tamas to be born of ignorance, deluding all embodied beings; it binds fast, O Arjuna, through heedlessness, indolence, and sleep.

lessons.text: Notice which force is driving you right now.
lessons.rationale: Each guna attaches differently and takes turns dominating; the forces are always in flux within you.
lessons.old_text: Notice one force overpower the others, then shift.

verses.text: Sattva attaches to happiness, Rajas to action, O Arjuna, while Tamas, verily shrouding knowledge, attaches to heedlessness.
Now, O Arjuna, Sattva prevails, having overpowered Rajas and Tamas; then Rajas, having overpowered Sattva and Tamas; and then Tamas, having overpowered Sattva and Rajas.

lessons.text: When insight sharpens, clarity is leading you.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of sattva predominant: wisdom-light shining through every gate of the body.
lessons.old_text: When insight lights every sense, harmony is guiding you.

verses.text: When the wisdom-light shines through every gate of this body, then it may be known that Sattva is predominant.

lessons.text: When greed and restlessness surge, craving is driving you.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of rajas predominant: greed, activity, restlessness, and longing.
lessons.old_text: When greed and restlessness surge, passion is driving you.

verses.text: Greed, activity, the undertaking of actions, restlessness, and longing—these arise when Rajas is predominant, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: When fog and laziness settle, inertia has taken hold.
lessons.rationale: Diagnostic sign of tamas predominant: darkness, inertia, carelessness, and delusion.
lessons.old_text: When fog and laziness settle, inertia has taken hold.

verses.text: Darkness, inertia, carelessness, and delusion—these arise when Tamas is predominant, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: The mood that rules you shapes where you end up.
lessons.rationale: Each guna dominant at death leads to a different destiny: pure worlds, rebirth among the active, or the womb of the deluded.
lessons.old_text: Know the force ruling at death shapes your rebirth.

verses.text: If the embodied one meets death when Sattva is predominant, then they attain the spotless worlds of the knowers of the Highest.
Meeting death in Rajas, he is born among those who are attached to action; and dying in Tamas, he is born in the womb of the thoughtless.

lessons.text: Every act bears fruit: clarity, pain, or dullness.
lessons.rationale: The fruits of the gunas differ: sattva yields knowledge, rajas yields pain and greed, tamas yields ignorance.
lessons.old_text: Weigh each act's fruit: clarity, pain, or dullness.

verses.text: They say that the fruit of good action is Sattvic and pure; indeed, the fruit of Rajas is pain, and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance.
From Sattva arises knowledge, and greed from Rajas; heedlessness and delusion arise from Tamas, and also ignorance.

lessons.text: Your ruling mood lifts, holds, or sinks you.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic natures ascend, rajasic dwell in the middle, tamasic descend.
lessons.old_text: Your ruling force lifts, holds, or sinks you.

verses.text: Those seated in Sattva ascend; those of Rajasic nature dwell in the middle; and those of Tamasic nature, abiding in the function of the lowest Guna, descend.

lessons.text: See the forces at work, then rise above them.
lessons.rationale: The seer who beholds no agent but the gunas, and knows what is higher, transcends them to immortality.
lessons.old_text: See only the gunas acting, then rise beyond them.

verses.text: When the seer beholds no agent other than the Gunas and knows that which is higher than them, he attains to My Being.
The embodied one, having crossed beyond these three Gunas from which the body is evolved, is freed from birth, death, decay, and pain, and attains immortality.

lessons.text: Stay steady whether moods rise or fade.
lessons.rationale: The marks of one who has transcended the gunas: neither hating nor longing, equal in pleasure and pain, praise and blame, friend and foe.
lessons.old_text: Stay unmoved whether the forces arise or fade.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said, "When light, activity, and delusion are present, he does not hate them, nor does he long for them when they are absent.
He who, seated like one unconcerned, is not moved by the dualities, and who, knowing that the dualities are active, is self-centered and does not move.

Who is the same in pleasure and pain, who dwells in the Self, to whom a clod of earth, a stone, and gold are all alike, who is the same to the dear and the unfriendly, who is firm, and to whom censure and praise are one and the same.
Who is the same in honor and dishonor, the same to friend and foe, abandoning all undertakings, he is said to have transcended the dualities.

lessons.text: Steady commitment carries you beyond your moods.
lessons.rationale: Unwavering devotion is the means to cross beyond the gunas and become fit for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Serve with steady devotion to cross beyond them.

verses.text: And he who serves Me with unwavering devotion, he, crossing beyond the dualities, is fit for becoming Brahman.

skipped.reason: Arjuna's framing question asking for the marks of the transcender; the answer that follows (22-25) carries the teaching.

lessons.text: Cut an unhealthy attachment at its root.
lessons.rationale: The upside-down cosmic tree of worldly existence, felled with the strong axe of non-attachment.
lessons.old_text: Cut the tangled tree of attachment at its root.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said: They (the wise) speak of the indestructible peepul tree, with its roots above and branches below, whose leaves are the meters or hymns; he who knows it is a knower of the Vedas.
Its branches spread below and above, nourished by the Gunas; its buds are sense-objects, and its roots stretch forth below in the world of men, originating action.
Its form is not perceived here as such, nor its end, origin, foundation, or resting place; having cut asunder this firmly rooted peepul tree with the strong axe of non-attachment.

lessons.text: Rise above pride, delusion, and the pull of opposites.
lessons.rationale: The undeluded conquer attachment and the pairs of pleasure and pain to reach the eternal.
lessons.old_text: Rise above pride, delusion, and the pull of opposites.

verses.text: Free from pride and delusion, victorious over the evil of attachment, dwelling constantly in the Self, their desires having completely turned away, freed from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, they, the undeluded, reach the eternal goal.

lessons.text: Understand what matters and right action follows.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the highest Purusha makes one wise and accomplishes all duties.
lessons.old_text: Know the supreme Self and your duty fulfills itself.

verses.text: He who, undeluded, knows Me as the highest Purusha, he, knowing all, worships Me with his whole being (heart), O Arjuna.
Thus, I have imparted to you this most secret science, O sinless one; by knowing this, one becomes wise, and all their duties are accomplished, O Arjuna.

skipped.reason: Purely metaphysical description of the supreme abode beyond sun, moon, and fire; no distinct practical takeaway.

lessons.text: Face danger without fear, and keep your heart clean.
lessons.rationale: Opens the divine list with fearlessness and purity of heart (16:1).
lessons.old_text: Meet danger fearlessly and keep your heart pure.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga, almsgiving, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity, and straightforwardness.

lessons.text: Master your impulses; give without holding back.
lessons.rationale: Sense-control and almsgiving named among divine endowments in 16:1.
lessons.old_text: Master your senses and give without holding back.

verses.text: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga, almsgiving, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity, and straightforwardness.

lessons.text: Tell the truth. Harm nothing.
lessons.rationale: Harmlessness and truth head the second stanza of virtues (16:2).
lessons.old_text: Speak the truth and harm no living thing.

verses.text: Harmlessness, truth, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, absence of crookedness, compassion for beings, non-covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and absence of fickleness.

lessons.text: Let anger go. Stay gentle, want less.
lessons.rationale: Absence of anger, non-covetousness, and gentleness from 16:2, distinct from truth/non-violence.
lessons.old_text: Let anger go; stay gentle and free of greed.

verses.text: Harmlessness, truth, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, absence of crookedness, compassion for beings, non-covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and absence of fickleness.

lessons.text: Forgive quickly. Carry no grudge, no pride.
lessons.rationale: Forgiveness, absence of hatred and pride mark the divine-born nature (16:3).
lessons.old_text: Forgive readily and carry no hatred or pride.

verses.text: Vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of hatred, absence of pride—these belong to one born for a divine state, O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Good character frees you; cruelty traps you.
lessons.rationale: 16:4-6 contrast the two natures: divine leads to liberation, demonic to bondage; framing verses grouped.
lessons.old_text: Choose the divine path; it frees, the demonic binds.

verses.text: Hypocrisy, arrogance, and self-conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance—these belong to one who is born for a demoniacal state, O Partha.
The divine nature is deemed conducive to liberation, and the demonic to bondage. Grieve not, O Arjuna, for you are born with divine endowments.
There are two types of beings in this world: the divine and the demoniacal. The divine has been described at length; hear from Me, O Arjuna, about the demoniacal.

lessons.text: Know what to do, and what to leave alone.
lessons.rationale: The demonic fail to discern right action and refrain (16:7).
lessons.old_text: Know what to do and what to shun.

verses.text: The demoniacal do not know what to do and what to refrain from; they have neither purity, nor right conduct, nor truth.

lessons.text: Don't treat the world as meaningless.
lessons.rationale: The demonic worldview denies truth, morality, and God, breeding destruction (16:8-9).
lessons.old_text: Don't dismiss the world as meaningless and godless.

verses.text: They say, "This universe is without truth, without a moral basis, without a God, brought about by mutual union, with lust as its cause; what else?"
Holding this view, these ruined souls of small intellect and fierce deeds come forth as enemies of the world, intent on its destruction.

lessons.text: Drop the pretense and the endless chasing.
lessons.rationale: Insatiable desire, hypocrisy, and anxious hoarding for lust described across 16:10-12.
lessons.old_text: Drop hypocrisy and the endless chase after craving.

verses.text: Filled with insatiable desires, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding evil ideas due to delusion, they work with impure intentions.
Giving themselves over to immeasurable cares that end only with death, regarding the gratification of lust as their highest aim, and feeling sure that that is all.
Bound by a hundred ties of hope, given over to lust and anger, they strive to obtain hoards of wealth by unlawful means for sensual enjoyment.

lessons.text: Your possessions aren't proof of your worth.
lessons.rationale: The demonic boast of acquisitions, conquests, and status (16:13-15).
lessons.old_text: Stop counting gains as proof of your greatness.

verses.text: "I have gained this today; I will fulfill this desire of mine; this is mine, and this wealth will be mine in the future."
"I have slain that enemy, and I shall slay others too. I am the Lord; I enjoy, I am perfect, powerful, and happy."
"I am wealthy and born into a noble family. Who is my equal? I shall perform sacrifices, give charity, and rejoice," thus deluded by ignorance.

lessons.text: Don't let fantasy drag you under.
lessons.rationale: Bewildered by fancies and snared by delusion, they fall into hell (16:16).
lessons.old_text: Untangle from fancies before delusion drags you down.

verses.text: Bewildered by many fancies, entangled in the snare of delusion, addicted to the gratification of lust, they fall into a foul hell.

lessons.text: Don't make a show of your good acts.
lessons.rationale: Ostentatious, name-only sacrifices contrary to scripture (16:17).
lessons.old_text: Never worship for show or to be seen.

verses.text: Self-conceited, stubborn, filled with pride and intoxication of wealth, they perform sacrifices in name only for ostentation, contrary to scriptural ordinances.

lessons.text: Arrogance curdles into cruelty. Drop it.
lessons.rationale: Egoism and malice breed hatred and a downward spiral of rebirth (16:18-20).
lessons.old_text: Let go of arrogance that turns you hateful.

verses.text: Given over to egoism, power, haughtiness, lust, and anger, these malicious people hate Me in their own bodies and in the bodies of others.
Those cruel haters, the worst among men in the world, I hurl those evil-doers into the wombs of demons only.
Entering into demoniacal wombs and deluded, birth after birth, they do not attain Me, thus falling, O Arjuna, into a condition still lower than that.

lessons.text: Shut the three gates of ruin: lust, anger, greed.
lessons.rationale: The signature teaching of the chapter: three gates to hell (16:21).
lessons.old_text: Shut the three gates of ruin: lust, anger, greed.

verses.text: There are three gates to this hell, destructive of the self: lust, anger, and greed; therefore, one should abandon these three.

lessons.text: Free of those, do what truly serves you.
lessons.rationale: Liberation from the three gates lets you pursue the beneficial and reach the goal (16:22).
lessons.old_text: Freed from those gates, do what truly benefits you.

verses.text: A person who is liberated from these three gates of darkness, O Arjuna, practices what is beneficial for them and thus goes to the Supreme Goal.

lessons.text: Don't let impulse override hard-won wisdom.
lessons.rationale: Acting on desire while casting aside scripture yields no perfection (16:23).
lessons.old_text: Don't let raw impulse override tested wisdom.

verses.text: He who, having cast aside the ordinances of the scriptures, acts under the impulse of desire, does not attain perfection, nor happiness, nor the Supreme Goal.

lessons.text: Let sound principles decide your actions.
lessons.rationale: Make scripture your authority for right and wrong action (16:24).
lessons.old_text: Let sound principles decide what you should do.

verses.text: Therefore, let the scripture be thy authority in determining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done. Having known what is said in the ordinance of the scriptures, thou shouldst act in this world.

lessons.text: You become what you believe.
lessons.rationale: 17:3 states the chapter's thesis directly: a person is made of their faith; as their faith is, so are they.
lessons.old_text: You become the faith you hold.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "What is the condition of those who, disregarding the injunctions of the scriptures, perform sacrifice with faith—is it Sattva, Rajas, or Tamas, O Krishna?"
The Blessed Lord said, "There are threefold faiths inherent in the nature of the embodied: the sattvic (pure), the rajasic (passionate), and the tamasic (dark). Hear of them."
The faith of each is in accordance with their nature, O Arjuna. People consist of their faith; as a person's faith is, so are they.
The sattvic, or pure, men worship the gods; the rajasic, or passionate, worship the yakshas and rakshasas; the others, the tamasic or deluded people, worship ghosts and hosts of nature-spirits.

lessons.text: Don't punish yourself to prove your discipline.
lessons.rationale: 17:5-6 condemn austerities born of hypocrisy and lust that senselessly torment body and spirit.
lessons.old_text: Don't torture yourself with ego-driven, harsh discipline.

verses.text: Those men who practice terrific austerities not prescribed by the scriptures, given to hypocrisy and egoism, driven by the force of lust and attachment.
Know thou these to be of demonical resolves, senselessly torturing all the elements in the body and Me who dwell in the body.

lessons.text: Eat what brings you health, strength, and calm.
lessons.rationale: 17:8 describes sattvic food that increases life, purity, health, and joy.
lessons.old_text: Eat wholesome food that brings health, strength, and cheer.

verses.text: The foods that increase life, purity, strength, health, joy, and cheerfulness (good appetite), which are savory, oily, substantial, and agreeable, are dear to the Sattvic (pure) people.

lessons.text: Skip food that overstimulates and leaves you wrecked.
lessons.rationale: 17:9 names rajasic foods (bitter, sour, burning) that produce pain, grief, and disease.
lessons.old_text: Avoid harsh, overstimulating food that breeds pain and disease.

verses.text: The foods that are bitter, sour, salty, overly hot, pungent, dry, and burning are liked by the Rajasic and are productive of pain, grief, and disease.

lessons.text: Refuse what's stale, spoiled, and lifeless.
lessons.rationale: 17:10 describes tamasic food that is stale, putrid, and impure.
lessons.old_text: Refuse stale, spoiled, and lifeless food.

verses.text: That which is stale, tasteless, putrid, rotten, rejected, and impure is the food liked by the Tamasic.

lessons.text: Do good because it's right, not for reward.
lessons.rationale: 17:11 defines sattvic sacrifice: offered as duty, without desire for reward.
lessons.old_text: Do your work as sacred duty, not for reward.

verses.text: That sacrifice which is offered by men without desire for reward, as enjoined by the ordinance (scripture), with a firm faith that doing so is their duty, is Sattvic or pure.

lessons.text: Good done for reward and display is hollow.
lessons.rationale: 17:12 marks sacrifice done for reward and display as rajasic.
lessons.old_text: Never perform good deeds merely for show.

verses.text: The sacrifice that is offered, O Arjuna, seeking a reward and for show, know that to be a Rajasic Yajna.

lessons.text: Respect what's wise; keep yourself clean and gentle.
lessons.rationale: 17:14 lists austerity of the body: reverence for the wise, purity, straightforwardness, non-injury.
lessons.old_text: Honor your teachers and keep your body pure and gentle.

verses.text: Worship of the gods, the twice-born, the teachers, and the wise; purity, straightforwardness, celibacy, and non-injury are all called the austerities of the body.

lessons.text: Speak what's true, kind, and calm.
lessons.rationale: 17:15 defines austerity of speech: truthful, pleasant, beneficial, causing no agitation.
lessons.old_text: Speak words that are true, kind, and calming.

verses.text: Speech that causes no excitement, is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial; the practice of studying the Vedas is called austerity of speech.

lessons.text: Steady your mind with calm and self-control.
lessons.rationale: 17:16 defines mental austerity: serenity, good-heartedness, self-control, purity of nature.
lessons.old_text: Steady your mind with serenity and self-control.

verses.text: Serenity of mind, good-heartedness, self-control, and purity of nature—this is called mental austerity.

lessons.text: Discipline done for applause won't last.
lessons.rationale: 17:18 marks austerity done for honor and reception, with hypocrisy, as rajasic and unstable.
lessons.old_text: Don't practice discipline just to win praise.

verses.text: The austerity that is practiced with the aim of gaining good reception, honor, and worship, and with hypocrisy, is said to be Rajasic, unstable, and transient.

lessons.text: Never turn discipline into self-harm or cruelty.
lessons.rationale: 17:19 condemns tamasic austerity practiced through self-torture or to destroy another.
lessons.old_text: Never turn discipline into self-torture or harm to others.

verses.text: That austerity which is practised out of a foolish notion, with self-torture, or for the purpose of destroying another, is declared to be of the Tamasic nature.

lessons.text: Give to those who need it, expecting nothing back.
lessons.rationale: 17:20 defines sattvic giving: to a worthy person, at a fitting place and time, expecting nothing back.
lessons.old_text: Give as a duty to those who truly deserve it.

verses.text: That gift which is given to one who does nothing in return, knowing it to be a duty to give in a suitable place and time to a worthy person, is held to be Sattvic.

lessons.text: Give generously, never grudgingly or for payback.
lessons.rationale: 17:21 marks giving that expects a reward or is done reluctantly as rajasic.
lessons.old_text: Give generously, never grudgingly or for return.

verses.text: And, that gift which is given with the intention of receiving something in return, or expecting a reward, or begrudgingly, is considered to be Rajasic.

lessons.text: Give with respect, at the right time and place.
lessons.rationale: 17:22 condemns gifts given at the wrong time and place, to the unworthy, with insult.
lessons.old_text: Give with respect, at the right place and time.

verses.text: The gift that is given in the wrong place and at the wrong time, to unworthy persons, without respect or with insult, is declared to be of a Tamasic nature.

lessons.text: Without conviction, your effort comes to nothing.
lessons.rationale: 17:28 closes the chapter: whatever is done without faith is 'Asat', of no value here or hereafter.
lessons.old_text: Act with faith, or your effort comes to nothing.

verses.text: Whatever is sacrificed, given, or performed, and whatever austerity is practiced without faith, it is called 'Asat', O Arjuna; it is of no value here or hereafter (after death).

skipped.reason: Announces the threefold division of food, sacrifice, austerity, and gift — a framing statement developed in the following verses.
Restates faithless (tamasic) sacrifice; its practical teaching is captured by the 'act with faith' lesson from 17:28.
Summarizes the threefold austerity done with faith and no reward — already covered by the body, speech, and mind lessons plus the duty lesson.
Om Tat Sat etymology / ritual origin, not a practical directive.
Ritual instruction to begin acts with 'Om' — etymological, skipped per guidance.
Ritual use of 'Tat'; its 'without aiming for fruits' sense is already covered by the duty lesson from 17:11.
Etymology of the word 'Sat' — definitional, not a practical lesson.
Etymological explanation of 'Sat' applied to steadfast action — ritual framing, skipped per guidance.

lessons.text: Give up the craving, not the work.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's core distinction: sannyasa renounces desire-driven action, tyaga abandons the fruits.
lessons.old_text: Renounce selfish desire, not the action itself.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "O mighty-armed Hrishikesa, I desire to know the essence or truth of renunciation and abandonment severally, O slayer of Kesi."
The Blessed Lord said, "The sages understand sannyasa to be the renunciation of action with desire; the wise declare the abandonment of the fruits of all actions to be tyaga."

lessons.text: Don't abandon the practices that better you.
lessons.rationale: These purifying acts should never be abandoned but continued.
lessons.old_text: Keep performing charity, sacrifice, and austerity.

verses.text: Acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be abandoned, but should be performed; for sacrifice, gift, and austerity are the purifiers of the wise.

lessons.text: Even good work, do it without clinging to results.
lessons.rationale: Perform good acts while dropping attachment and desire for results.
lessons.old_text: Do even worthy deeds without craving reward.

verses.text: But even these actions should be performed, leaving aside attachment and the desire for rewards, O Arjuna; this is my certain and most assured conviction.

lessons.text: Never quit your duty out of confusion.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing obligatory action from confusion is tamasic, not true renunciation.
lessons.old_text: Never abandon your duty out of delusion.

verses.text: Verily, the renunciation of obligatory action is not proper; the abandonment of the same out of delusion is declared to be Tamasic.

lessons.text: Don't quit hard work just to dodge discomfort.
lessons.rationale: Dropping action from fear of bodily trouble is mere rajasic renunciation, earning no merit.
lessons.old_text: Don't quit hard work to avoid discomfort.

verses.text: He who abandons action out of fear of bodily trouble (because it is painful), does not obtain the merit of renunciation by performing such Rajasic renunciation.

lessons.text: Do what's needed simply because it's right.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic renunciation: act because it ought to be done, without attachment or reward.
lessons.old_text: Do required work simply because it's right.

verses.text: Whatever obligatory action is done, O Arjuna, merely because it ought to be done, abandoning attachment and also the desire for reward, that renunciation is regarded as sattvic (pure).

lessons.text: Don't dread hard tasks or crave easy ones.
lessons.rationale: The true renouncer hates no unpleasant task and clings to no pleasant one.
lessons.old_text: Neither dread hard tasks nor crave easy ones.

verses.text: The man of renunciation, pervaded by purity, intelligent, and with his doubts cut asunder, does not hate an unpleasant task nor is he attached to a pleasant one.

lessons.text: You can't stop acting, so release the results.
lessons.rationale: No embodied being can abandon all action; relinquishing rewards is real renunciation.
lessons.old_text: You cannot stop acting; release the fruits.

verses.text: Indeed, it is not possible for an embodied being to completely abandon actions; however, he who relinquishes the rewards of actions is truly called a man of renunciation.

lessons.text: Cling to outcomes and they own you.
lessons.rationale: The threefold fruit accrues after death to those who cling, never to renouncers.
lessons.old_text: Only the unattached escape action's binding fruits.

verses.text: The threefold fruit of action (evil, good, and mixed) accrues after death to those who do not abandon it, but never to those who do.

lessons.text: Many forces shape any outcome, not just you.
lessons.rationale: Body, doer, senses, functions, and the presiding deity are the five causes of every deed.
lessons.old_text: Know that many factors, not you alone, drive action.

verses.text: Learn from Me, O mighty-armed Arjuna, these five causes, as declared in the Sankhya system, for the accomplishment of all actions.
The body, the doer, the various senses, the different functions of various kinds, and the presiding deity—the fifth.
Whatever action a person performs with their body, speech, and mind, whether right or wrong, these five are its causes.

lessons.text: Stop taking sole credit for what happens.
lessons.rationale: The untrained mind wrongly views the isolated Self as the agent.
lessons.old_text: Stop seeing yourself as the sole doer.

verses.text: Now, such being the case, verily he who, owing to an untrained understanding, looks upon his Self, which is isolated, as the agent, he of perverted intelligence does not see.

lessons.text: Act without ego, and nothing binds you.
lessons.rationale: Free of the egoistic notion, one is not bound even by great action.
lessons.old_text: Act free of ego and remain unbound.

verses.text: He who is free from the egoistic notion, whose intelligence is not tainted by good or evil, though he slays these people, he does not slay, nor is he bound by the action.

lessons.text: See the same thread running through everyone.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic knowledge perceives the undivided Reality present in every being.
lessons.old_text: See one indestructible reality within all beings.

verses.text: That by which one sees the indestructible Reality in all beings, not separate in any of them—know that knowledge to be Sattvic.

lessons.text: Don't mistake one piece for the whole picture.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic knowledge clings to a single trivial effect as if it were everything.
lessons.old_text: Don't mistake one fragment for the whole.

verses.text: But that which clings to one single effect as if it were the whole, without reason, without any foundation in Truth, and is trivial—that is declared to be Tamasic.

lessons.text: Do your duty without craving, favoritism, or spite.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic action is ordained, unattached, and free of desire for reward.
lessons.old_text: Do prescribed work without love, hate, or gain.

verses.text: An action that is ordained, free from attachment, done without love or hatred, and without desire for reward is declared to be Sattvic.

lessons.text: Weigh the cost and your limits before acting.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic action ignores loss, injury, and one's own capacity.
lessons.old_text: Weigh consequences and your ability before acting.

verses.text: That action which is undertaken from delusion, without regard for the consequences, loss, injury, and one's own ability, is declared to be Tamasic (dark).

lessons.text: Work with resolve, unmoved by win or loss.
lessons.rationale: The sattvic doer is firm and enthusiastic, unaffected by outcome.
lessons.old_text: Work with resolve, unmoved by success or failure.

verses.text: An agent who is free from attachment, non-egoistic, endowed with firmness and enthusiasm, and unaffected by success or failure, is considered to be of a Sattvic (pure) nature.

lessons.text: Root out laziness, deceit, and delay.
lessons.rationale: These mark the tamasic doer; naming them helps you shed them.
lessons.old_text: Root out laziness, deceit, and procrastination.

verses.text: Unsteady, vulgar, inflexible, deceitful, malicious, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating—such an agent is called Tamasic.

lessons.text: Know clearly what to pursue and what to drop.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic intellect knows action from inaction, bondage from liberation.
lessons.old_text: Discern clearly what to do and avoid.

verses.text: The intellect which knows the path of work and renunciation, what should be done and what should not be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation—that intellect is Sattvic (pure), O Arjuna.

lessons.text: Don't let confusion make wrong look right.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic intellect, wrapped in darkness, mistakes adharma for dharma.
lessons.old_text: Never let darkness call wrong things right.

verses.text: That intellect, O Arjuna, which is enveloped in darkness and sees Adharma as Dharma and all things perverted, is Tamasic (dark).

lessons.text: Steady your mind, your breath, your senses.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic firmness restrains mind, life-force, and senses by unwavering yoga.
lessons.old_text: Steady your mind, breath, and senses through discipline.

verses.text: The unwavering firmness, through which Yoga restrains the functions of the mind, life-force, and senses—that firmness, O Arjuna, is Sattvic (pure).

lessons.text: Stop clinging to fear, grief, and laziness.
lessons.rationale: Tamasic firmness refuses to let go of these; releasing them frees you.
lessons.old_text: Stop clinging to sleep, fear, grief, and despair.

verses.text: That firmness, O Arjuna, by which a stupid man does not abandon sleep, fear, grief, despair, and conceit, is Tamasic.

lessons.text: Choose the joy that's hard first, sweet later.
lessons.rationale: Sattvic happiness is poison at first, nectar in the end, born of self-realization.
lessons.old_text: Choose joy that starts bitter but ends sweet.

verses.text: That which is like poison at first but in the end like nectar—that happiness is declared to be sattvic, born of the purity of one's own mind due to self-realization.

lessons.text: Beware pleasures sweet now, bitter later.
lessons.rationale: Rajasic happiness from sense contact begins as nectar and ends as poison.
lessons.old_text: Beware pleasures sweet now but poisonous later.

verses.text: That happiness which arises from the contact of the senses with the objects, which is initially like nectar but eventually like poison, is said to be Rajasic.

lessons.text: Let your natural strengths shape your work.
lessons.rationale: Duties are apportioned according to the qualities born of one's own nature.
lessons.old_text: Let your natural qualities shape your work.

verses.text: Of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaisyas, and Sudras, O Arjuna, the duties are distributed according to the qualities born of their own nature.
Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness, and uprightness, as well as knowledge, realization, and belief in God, are the duties of Brahmanas, born of their own nature.
Prowess, splendor, firmness, dexterity, and not fleeing from battle, generosity, and lordliness are the duties of the Kshatriyas, born of their own nature.
Agriculture, cattle-rearing, and trade are the duties of the Vaisya (merchant), born of their own nature; and service is the duty of the Sudra (servant-class), born of their own nature.

lessons.text: You grow most by mastering your own work.
lessons.rationale: Each person attains perfection by being devoted to their own duty.
lessons.old_text: Reach perfection through devotion to your own work.

verses.text: Each person devoted to their own duty attains perfection. How they attain perfection while being engaged in their own duty, hear now.

lessons.text: Pour your whole heart into ordinary work.
lessons.rationale: Worshipping the source of all through one's own duty leads to perfection.
lessons.old_text: Turn your everyday work into worship.

verses.text: He from whom all the beings have evolved and by whom all this is pervaded, worshipping Him with his own duty, one attains perfection.

lessons.text: Better your own path done badly than another's done well.
lessons.rationale: One's own dharma, though flawed, is better than another's performed well.
lessons.old_text: Do your own duty poorly over another's well.

verses.text: Better is one's own duty, even if it is destitute of merits, than the duty of another well performed. He who does the duty ordained by his own nature incurs no sin.

lessons.text: Every job has flaws; don't quit yours over them.
lessons.rationale: Every undertaking has some fault, as fire has smoke; keep to your inborn duty.
lessons.old_text: Don't abandon your calling for its flaws.

verses.text: One should not, O Arjuna, abandon the duty to which one is born, though it may be faulty; for, all undertakings are enveloped by evil, just as fire is by smoke.

lessons.text: Master your wants, and action stops weighing on you.
lessons.rationale: Unattached intellect and self-mastery yield the supreme state of actionlessness.
lessons.old_text: Subdue desire to find freedom in action.

verses.text: He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, who has subdued his self, from whom desire has fled, he attains the supreme state of freedom from action through renunciation.

lessons.text: Clear your head; let go of craving and hate.
lessons.rationale: A pure intellect controls the self and relinquishes attraction and aversion.
lessons.old_text: Purify your intellect and drop attraction and hatred.

verses.text: Endowed with a pure intellect, controlling the self through firmness, relinquishing sound and other objects and abandoning attraction and hatred.

lessons.text: Live simply, speak less, sit with yourself.
lessons.rationale: Solitude, sparing food, subdued speech, and constant meditation prepare one for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Live simply, speak little, and meditate steadily.

verses.text: Dwelling in solitude, eating sparingly, with speech, body, and mind subdued, always engaged in meditation and concentration, and resorting to dispassion.

lessons.text: Let go of ego, arrogance, and 'mine.'
lessons.rationale: Abandoning egoism, arrogance, and possessiveness makes one fit for Brahman.
lessons.old_text: Release ego, arrogance, and the sense of 'mine'.

verses.text: Having abandoned egoism, strength, arrogance, desire, anger, and covetousness, and being free from the notion of 'mine' and peaceful, he is fit for becoming Brahman.

lessons.text: Grow calm, and treat everyone as equal.
lessons.rationale: Serene in the Self, one neither grieves nor desires and treats all equally.
lessons.old_text: Grow serene and regard all beings alike.

verses.text: Becoming Brahman, serene in the Self, he neither grieves nor desires; he is the same to all beings, and obtains supreme devotion to Me.

lessons.text: Devote yourself fully, and real understanding follows.
lessons.rationale: By devotion one truly knows the Lord and enters the Supreme.
lessons.old_text: Know the divine through wholehearted devotion.

verses.text: By devotion, he knows Me in truth, who and what I am; then, having known Me in truth, he immediately enters into the Supreme.

lessons.text: Do everything, but stay anchored to something steady.
lessons.rationale: Performing all actions while sheltered in the divine grants the eternal state.
lessons.old_text: Take refuge while still doing all your work.

verses.text: Having taken refuge in Me and doing all actions, by My grace he obtains the eternal, indestructible state of being.

lessons.text: Act fully, then let go of what you did.
lessons.rationale: Mentally renounce all deeds in the Lord and fix the mind on Him.
lessons.old_text: Offer every action mentally to the divine.

verses.text: Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, having Me as the highest goal, and resorting to the yoga of discrimination, do thou ever fix thy mind on Me.

lessons.text: Drop the ego, and the obstacles shrink.
lessons.rationale: Fixing the mind on the divine overcomes obstacles; egoism leads to ruin.
lessons.old_text: Surrender your ego to overcome every obstacle.

verses.text: Fixing your mind on Me, you shall, by My grace, overcome all obstacles; but if you will not hear Me due to egoism, you shall perish.

lessons.text: Your nature will act, whether your ego likes it or not.
lessons.rationale: Refusing to act from egoism is vain; one's own nature will drive the deed anyway.
lessons.old_text: Your nature compels what your ego refuses.

verses.text: If, filled with egoism, thou thinkest, "I will not fight," then thy resolve is vain; nature will compel thee.
O Arjuna, bound by your own Karma (action) born of your own nature, that which from delusion you wish not to do, even that you shall do helplessly.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully, and peace follows.
lessons.rationale: Taking full refuge in the Lord yields supreme peace and the eternal abode.
lessons.old_text: Surrender with your whole being to find peace.

verses.text: Fly to Him for refuge with all your being, O Arjuna; by His grace you will obtain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

lessons.text: Think it through, then decide for yourself.
lessons.rationale: Krishna gives the secret wisdom, then leaves Arjuna free to reflect and act.
lessons.old_text: Reflect deeply, then choose your own path.

verses.text: Thus, wisdom more secret than secrecy itself has been declared to you by me. Reflect on it fully, then act as you wish.

lessons.text: Fix your focus, and you'll get there.
lessons.rationale: Devote your mind, worship, and bow, and you will reach the Lord.
lessons.old_text: Fix your mind on the divine and arrive.

verses.text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. You will come to Me; I truly promise you this, for you are dear to Me.

lessons.text: Do your best, then stop clinging to the rest.
lessons.rationale: The surrender climax: abandon all duties, take sole refuge, and be freed from sin.
lessons.old_text: Let go of everything, take refuge, stop grieving.

verses.text: Abandon all duties and take refuge in Me alone; I will liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.

lessons.text: Don't force advice on those who won't hear it.
lessons.rationale: Never teach this to the unwilling; teaching devotees with love is dearest of all.
lessons.old_text: Share sacred wisdom only with the receptive.

verses.text: Never speak this to one who is devoid of austerities or devotion, who does not render service, who does not desire to listen, or who cavils at Me.
He who, with supreme devotion to Me, teaches this supreme secret to My devotees, shall undoubtedly come to Me.
There is no one among men who does service dearer to Me, nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him.

lessons.text: Listen openly, without a hostile heart.
lessons.rationale: Hearing it faithfully and without malice leads to liberation and happy worlds.
lessons.old_text: Listen with faith and free from malice.

verses.text: Also, the man who hears this, full of faith and free from malice, shall attain to the happy worlds of those of righteous deeds, and be liberated.

lessons.text: Listen with full attention, and confusion clears.
lessons.rationale: Krishna asks whether one-pointed attention has destroyed delusion.
lessons.old_text: Listen with full focus to dispel ignorance.

verses.text: Has this been heard, O Arjuna, with one-pointed focus? Has the delusion of your ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?

lessons.text: Let clarity return, then act without hesitation.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna's delusion is gone, his doubts dissolved, and he resolves to act.
lessons.old_text: Let clarity return and act without doubt.

verses.text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, for I have gained my knowledge (memory) through Your grace, O Krishna. I am now free from doubts. I will act according to Your word."

id_text.276: Refuse what's stale, spoiled, and lifeless.
id_text.277: Do good because it's right, not for reward.
id_text.278: Good done for reward and display is hollow.
id_text.279: Respect what's wise; keep yourself clean and gentle.
id_text.280: Speak what's true, kind, and calm.
id_text.281: Steady your mind with calm and self-control.
id_text.282: Discipline done for applause won't last.
id_text.283: Never turn discipline into self-harm or cruelty.
id_text.284: Give to those who need it, expecting nothing back.
id_text.285: Give generously, never grudgingly or for payback.
id_text.286: Give with respect, at the right time and place.
id_text.287: Without conviction, your effort comes to nothing.
id_text.288: Give up the craving, not the work.
id_text.289: Don't abandon the practices that better you.
id_text.290: Even good work, do it without clinging to results.
id_text.291: Never quit your duty out of confusion.
id_text.292: Don't quit hard work just to dodge discomfort.
id_text.293: Do what's needed simply because it's right.
id_text.294: Don't dread hard tasks or crave easy ones.
id_text.295: You can't stop acting, so release the results.
id_text.296: Cling to outcomes and they own you.
id_text.297: Many forces shape any outcome, not just you.
id_text.298: Stop taking sole credit for what happens.
id_text.299: Act without ego, and nothing binds you.
id_text.300: See the same thread running through everyone.
id_text.301: Don't mistake one piece for the whole picture.
id_text.302: Do your duty without craving, favoritism, or spite.
id_text.303: Weigh the cost and your limits before acting.
id_text.304: Work with resolve, unmoved by win or loss.
id_text.305: Root out laziness, deceit, and delay.
id_text.306: Know clearly what to pursue and what to drop.
id_text.307: Don't let confusion make wrong look right.
id_text.308: Steady your mind, your breath, your senses.
id_text.309: Stop clinging to fear, grief, and laziness.
id_text.310: Choose the joy that's hard first, sweet later.
id_text.311: Beware pleasures sweet now, bitter later.
id_text.312: Let your natural strengths shape your work.
id_text.313: You grow most by mastering your own work.
id_text.314: Pour your whole heart into ordinary work.
id_text.315: Better your own path done badly than another's done well.
id_text.316: Every job has flaws; don't quit yours over them.
id_text.317: Master your wants, and action stops weighing on you.
id_text.318: Clear your head; let go of craving and hate.
id_text.319: Live simply, speak less, sit with yourself.
id_text.320: Let go of ego, arrogance, and 'mine.'
id_text.321: Grow calm, and treat everyone as equal.
id_text.322: Devote yourself fully, and real understanding follows.
id_text.323: Do everything, but stay anchored to something steady.
id_text.324: Act fully, then let go of what you did.
id_text.325: Drop the ego, and the obstacles shrink.
id_text.326: Your nature will act, whether your ego likes it or not.
id_text.327: Give yourself fully, and peace follows.
id_text.328: Think it through, then decide for yourself.
id_text.329: Fix your focus, and you'll get there.
id_text.330: Do your best, then stop clinging to the rest.
id_text.331: Don't force advice on those who won't hear it.
id_text.332: Listen openly, without a hostile heart.
id_text.333: Listen with full attention, and confusion clears.
id_text.334: Let clarity return, then act without hesitation.
